Chromium Daily Summaries

Latest Chromium commits summarized daily

📅 View Weekly Summaries →

2026-05-17

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-17

Overview

This digest analyzes 7 commits, identifying one major breaking change. The most significant update involves stricter URI validation in Android's SelectFileDialog, which may impact embedders. Other changes include security hardening for the Wayland server, test infrastructure improvements, and minor UI and localization updates.

Android

  • ⚠️The SelectFileDialog public API now rejects scheme-less URIs and any URI scheme other than content://. This is a security hardening measure but is a breaking change for embedders who may have been passing other valid URI types, such as those with a file:// scheme, which will now fail. (a652043)
    AndroidUISecurity

Security

  • A potential buffer overflow in the Wayland server implementation was fixed by replacing a raw C-style array with std::array for improved memory safety. (16cb4d0)
    SecurityLinuxCompositor

Testing

  • Test suites for Sync and Signin utilities have been refactored to remove dependencies on the heavy-weight BrowserWithTestWindowTest fixture, improving test performance and reducing complexity. (d018942) (6ca077d)
    TestingSyncProfiles
  • The internal WaitTool for automated testing now enforces a URL check for tabs it operates on. (5b8b5c3)
    TestingInfra

UI

  • A plural string for "Sharing n tabs" has been added to the contextual tasks and compose box UI to ensure the counter is displayed correctly for multiple tabs. (3f982b3)
    UI

Internationalization

  • Localization string files (.xtb) were updated across various components and platforms to align with the latest source strings. (ca327f2)
    I18n

2026-05-16

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-16

Overview

This digest covers 58 commits from the Chromium repository. The analysis focuses on changes impacting GPU, security, the web platform, UI, and internal refactoring. One major breaking change was identified in the GPU control list API, alongside several security hardening improvements and new feature implementations for the Web Speech API, PDF annotations, and canvas rendering.

GPU

  • ⚠️The GpuControlList::MakeDecision() method signature has been changed by removing the default value for the ignored_entries parameter. Embedders calling this public API method without explicitly providing this argument will now encounter compile-time errors. (c7427fc)
    GPUBase
  • Introduces more rigorous validation for plane metadata when populating VADRMPRIMESurfaceDescriptor in the VaapiWrapper. This includes verifying plane pitch and ensuring the plane's memory range is within the DMA-buf bounds, hardening the component against invalid data. (69d10d8)
    GPUMedia
  • The IOSurfaceIsWebGPUCompatible() helper function has been moved from //media to //ui/gfx/mac to allow its use from //gpu without creating a circular dependency. This is an internal refactoring to support upcoming changes in MappableBuffer. (59ad18d)
    GPUMediamacOS

Security

  • Hardens the WebUSB implementation on macOS against a Use-After-Free (UAF) vulnerability related to endpoint aliasing. The fix rejects claiming an interface if it advertises a duplicate endpoint address already mapped to a different interface, aligning Chromium's logic with libusb's behavior. (a74fd42)
    USBSecuritymacOS
  • Prevents potential shortcut hijacking in the XDG Global Shortcuts portal on Linux. Suggested accelerators for manifest-defined commands are now cleared if they conflict with reserved browser shortcuts (e.g., Ctrl+T), mitigating abuse while still allowing users to manually assign them. (39567c3)
    ExtensionsSecurityLinux
  • Fixes a potential Use-After-Free in the Linux printing portal (PrintDialogLinuxPortal) by replacing a raw aura::Window pointer with an aura::WindowTracker. This ensures the window's validity is checked after an asynchronous D-Bus call, preventing memory corruption if the window is destroyed during the operation. (2286275)
    PrintingSecurityLinux
  • Adds a bounds check to HidConnectionWin on Windows to harden against a potential buffer read overflow. This ensures that when stripping a leading zero byte from a feature report, the bytes_transferred count is validated against the buffer size. (0bac4a7)
    SecurityWindowsBrowser

Web Platform

  • Improves the performance of the Web Speech API by removing an artificial 2-second audio buffering delay in the on-device speech recognition engine. Audio chunks are now sent to the ASR stream immediately, significantly reducing the time-to-first-token for streaming dictation. (e31bc6d)
    BlinkPerformanceAI
  • Implements support for rendering <video> elements within a <canvas> using drawElement. The implementation synchronously reads video frame pixels and stores them in a pixel-backed image in the display list, enabling canvas pixel readback operations from a worker thread. (1d44c9d)
    BlinkHTMLMedia
  • Fixes a bug in WebCrypto CryptoKey serialization for the new ML-KEM and ML-DSA post-quantum algorithms. A new serialization tag was introduced to handle algorithms without key parameters, and new WPT tests were added to prevent future regressions. (23479ab) (dc989b8)
    BlinkCryptoSecurity

UI & UX

  • The audio processor for voice search has been significantly improved. It now uses a band-limited Root Mean Square (RMS) to specifically target human speech frequencies, incorporates adaptive noise tracking for better SNR, and applies dynamic cubic Bezier shaping to normalize audio levels. This results in a more responsive and accurate UI visualization for voice input. (43cbbf2)
    UIAI
  • On desktop, Omnibox suggestions are no longer pre-selected in a zero-prefix context (when the omnibox is empty). The first suggestion will only be pre-selected after the user starts typing. (7a891d0)
    UIBrowser
  • Improves native UI font rendering by correctly interpreting the emoji presentation selector (VS-16, U+FE0F). Text-default characters like ©, ®, and ™ followed by VS-16 will now correctly render using the platform's color emoji font in surfaces like tab titles, matching the behavior in web content. (1c45c78)
    UIFonts

PDF

  • Implements editing, deleting, and undo/redo for text annotations within the PDF Ink Signatures feature. This involves syncing state between the frontend and the PDFium backend, managing FPDF_PAGEOBJECT handles, and adding new client methods to discard and update text objects. (f05ce42) (b1809ed) (04cd755) (4f6437d) (feed479) (f880d93) (f469ee2)
    PDFUI

Android

  • The WebUI-based compose box is now enabled on Android (for desktop-class devices), bringing the feature to a new platform. The initial implementation excludes Lens features and the onboarding tooltip. (060cd97)
    AndroidUIAI
  • Introduces UI in Glic Settings on Android to indicate when the feature is managed by enterprise policy. (aad9316)
    AndroidEnterprisePolicy

Internals & Refactoring

  • The WebAppLaunchProcess has been refactored to use the central browser_navigator for all navigation paths. This consolidates navigation logic and simplifies the web app launch flow. (fe7265c)
    BrowserUI
  • The JSON structure of the chrome://web-app-internals debugging page has been normalized. The data is now a flat dictionary instead of an array of single-key objects, which allows for stronger typing and easier data access in the frontend. (f058bd2)
    DevToolsBrowser

2026-05-15

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-15

Overview

This summary covers 729 commits on the main branch, with 555 deemed relevant to developers. The changes span from May 15, 2026, starting with commit 5dae0b1af587f8b08c72b7ffa9b5acd5905cebb5 and ending with f98f0f4e339caeadfee03ffacdb1f1473b75b38c. The day was marked by an exceptionally high number of breaking changes across UI, core types, media, and platform-specific APIs, requiring significant attention from embedders.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public API for customizing colors on views::Textfield has been changed. Methods like SetBackgroundColor and SetTextColor which accepted an SkColor have been removed. Developers must now use the new methods that accept a std::optional<ui::ColorId>, such as SetTextColorId and SetSelectionBackgroundColorId. (b44a41b) (f20aaa7) (f262b03)
    UI
  • ⚠️Several core types and UI classes have been refactored. The RTTI class ui::FrameworkSpecificImplementation is now ui::SafeCastable (727bd8f7). The base::LanguageCode class no longer has a constexpr constructor to support variants (8805c200). The sql::DatabaseOptions::set_no_sync_on_wal_mode() method has been renamed to set_no_sync() (60871903). (727bd8f) (8805c20) (6087190)
    UIBaseStorage
  • ⚠️Two significant behavioral changes affect web applications. The engine now enforces stricter, spec-compliant parsing for ARIA roles, ignoring roles like listitem if they lack the required parent context (09e48bc9). Separately, WebUSB security has been hardened to block standard control transfers (e.g., SET_CONFIGURATION) via the generic controlTransfer method, requiring the use of dedicated WebIDL methods instead (3169e981). (09e48bc) (3169e98)
    BlinkAccessibilityUSB
  • ⚠️As part of a large-scale refactoring, public methods related to PWA display modes have been renamed from *Borderless* to *Unframed* (e.g., AppUsesBorderlessMode is now AppUsesUnframedMode) (5eb2a986). Additionally, the public method Browser::IsPointerLocked() has been removed; callers must now use the ExclusiveAccessManager (760506d2). (5eb2a98) (760506d)
    UIBrowser
  • ⚠️The media and graphics pipelines saw several breaking API changes. In viz, content_bounds was removed from OutputSurfaceFrame and the OverlayProcessorInterface::ProcessForOverlays signature was changed (bf710c23). The return type of media::AudioDecoderConfig::target_output_channel_layout() is now const ChannelLayoutConfig& (9a0e8b3a). The audio::SoundsManager singleton has been replaced by audio::GlobalSoundsManager (c2530960). (bf710c2) (9a0e8b3) (c253096)
    MediaCompositorGPU
  • ⚠️Multiple platform-specific APIs were changed. On Android, MediaRouteProvider::startObservingMediaSinks now requires an origin parameter (e66d0b06), and AutocompleteController::Stop now takes an AutocompleteStopReason enum (134822b9). On iOS, PaymentsAutofillClient::OnCardDataAvailable now requires an origin (73be2527). For ChromeOS, core timezone utilities were moved and their signatures changed (16c1f71f, d6b4bd51). (e66d0b0) (134822b) (73be252) (16c1f71) (d6b4bd5)
    AndroidiOSChromeOS
  • ⚠️Several service-level APIs were broken. The public method AutocompleteController::UpdateMatchDestinationURLWithInvocationSource was removed (99def36f). For Sync, SyncServiceCrypto::SetExplicitPassphraseDecryptionNigoriKey() (7542bc21) and SendTabToSelfModelObserver::SendTabToSelfModelLoaded() (7e872ba1) were removed. For networking, content::PrefetchUpdateHeadersParams was moved and renamed to network::HttpRequestHeadersUpdateParams (a89eecdf). (99def36) (7542bc2) (7e872ba) (a89eecd)
    AutofillSyncNetwork
  • ⚠️The enable_video_capture_service GN build argument has been removed, which may break Chromecast build configurations (3e0a630d). For external management servers, the EXTENSION_UPDATE_CHECK enum in device_management_backend.proto was renamed to BROWSER_EXTENSION_UPDATE_CHECK (ce1de7f6). For ChromeOS extensions, the searchSuggestEnabled property was removed from the file_manager_private API (a9bfde92). (3e0a630) (ce1de7f) (a9bfde9)
    BuildEnterpriseChromeOS

Security

  • Protections were added to disallow message connections from sandboxed extension pages (885bc095). Storage access levels are now correctly enforced for onChanged events originating from Chrome Sync or enterprise policy (19a50035). (885bc09) (19a5003)
    ExtensionsSecuritySync
  • Several vulnerabilities were addressed, including a fix for a compromised iframe injecting malicious nodes into a popup (5b5a413e), a potential data race in GPU D3D image backing (a0683f84), and a Use-After-Free in the Private State Token (PST) issuance helper (67439edd). The Web Bluetooth implementation was also updated to correctly check the blocklist for a descriptor's parent characteristic (a0b55d79). (5b5a413) (a0683f8) (67439ed) (a0b55d7)
    SecurityGPUBluetooth

Web Platform & APIs

  • The OpenH264 software encoder is now enabled by default on Android, providing a fallback for WebRTC, WebCodecs, and MediaRecorder (a4f5a3bd). In WebAudio, DirectConvolver and resamplers were updated to work with any input size, improving flexibility (76217b20). Additionally, the H264 decoder was optimized to prevent timeouts from large frame_num gaps in corrupted streams (c0cb19c6). (a4f5a3b) (76217b2) (c0cb19c)
    MediaWebRTCAndroid
  • The HTML Canvas drawElement method now throws an exception if an Element or ElementImage is drawn into a different canvas than the one it originated from. This improves consistency and security by preventing cross-canvas data leakage. (dbe9c63)
    BlinkHTMLSecurity
  • The ModulePreloadReferrer feature is now enabled by default (183f3e98). A resource leak in the Web Speech API when using a MediaStreamTrack has been fixed (b676377a). For WebXR, GL resource cleanup has been moved to pre-finalizers to prevent use-after-free errors during garbage collection (85f3563f). (183f3e9) (b676377) (85f3563)
    BlinkMediaXR

UI & UX

  • UI performance has been improved by preventing re-entrancy in browser layout and caching dialog anchor values to reduce redundant layout passes (4274c9f7, 19d5f27c). For GPU-accelerated rendering, the CompositorFrameSink is now created earlier for new renderers, reducing a browser-renderer roundtrip and improving responsiveness (227fa3e4). (4274c9f) (19d5f27) (227fa3e)
    PerformanceUICompositor
  • The Composebox and Omnibox components received numerous updates, including fixing IME interactions (7d6174f0), improving keyboard accessibility (d83072a5), and creating a dedicated ntp-composebox fork for the New Tab Page (3ddf82a4, 6466d1df). (7d6174f) (d83072a) (3ddf82a) (6466d1d)
    UIAI
  • The MenuModel API was enhanced to allow placing a minor icon to the right of minor text, enabling richer menu item displays (5fff9228). The download bubble UI has been further decoupled from BrowserView by using BrowserWindowInterface in its public API (371912c3). (5fff922) (371912c)
    UIDownloads

Platform Specific (Android, iOS, ChromeOS)

  • On Android, the kAndroidSmsOtpFilling feature for Autofill was enabled by default (67f85739). Startup performance is improved by deferring the creation of ContentView wrappers for frozen background tabs (3cbee391). Numerous UI refactorings occurred, including centralizing button management in the bottom bar (dc80a657) and implementing the UI layout for the Autofill 'Flyout Sheet' (e9b87bb9). (67f8573) (3cbee39) (dc80a65) (e9b87bb)
    AndroidAutofillPerformance
  • The iOS crash handler was fixed to prevent re-entry into partition alloc by caching a feature flag value at startup (5e528541). A bug in the AppBar's In-Product Help (IPH) was fixed by ensuring the presenter is retained until dismissed (d331699c). For builds using Blink, Perfetto tracing is now handled by the content layer instead of the legacy IOSTracingController (28a7ac5a). (5e52854) (d331699) (28a7ac5)
    iOSInfra

2026-05-14

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-14

Overview

On 2026-05-14, the main branch saw 597 commits, with 445 deemed relevant for this summary. This digest covers changes from commit 5e8bd89900b8 to 3eec1c723b47. The day was marked by a high number of significant breaking changes affecting core APIs like media::VideoFrame, PartitionAlloc, URLLoaderThrottle, and various Android and ChromeOS interfaces. Other notable updates include the stabilization of the CSS text-fit property, multiple security hardening improvements, and continued refactoring of Ash and iOS UI components.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The ycbcr_info() and set_ycbcr_info() methods have been removed from the core media::VideoFrame class. This information is now stored in media::VideoFrameMetadata. This is a major break for any code directly interacting with YCbCr info on video frames. (55b4406)
    Media
  • ⚠️Core PartitionAlloc headers (e.g., partition_root.h, partition_page.h) have been moved into an internal/ subdirectory. Embedders directly including these headers by their old paths will experience build failures. (7c766eb)
    BaseBuild
  • ⚠️The public API signature of blink::URLLoaderThrottle::BeforeWillRedirectRequest has changed. It removes three parameters and changes the net::RedirectInfo parameter from a pointer to a const reference. All custom URLLoaderThrottle implementations must be updated. (8c41713)
    BlinkNetwork
  • ⚠️The Android build configuration for resource optimization has been changed. Granular GN arguments (strip_resource_names, strip_unused_resources, etc.) have been removed and consolidated into a single optimize_resources argument, breaking build systems that relied on the old flags. (bc1ff55)
    AndroidBuild
  • ⚠️On Android, the getFilter() method has been removed from the public TabModelDelegate interface as part of a larger refactoring. Embedders who implement or call methods on TabModelDelegate will have build breaks. (73cd922)
    AndroidTabs
  • ⚠️The public Android SideUiObserver interface has been refactored. The onSideUiSpecsChanged() method's behavior was altered, and two new methods, onTransitionBegun() and onTransitionEnded(), were introduced. Implementers must update their code to handle the new interface. (c4e9467)
    AndroidUI
  • ⚠️The CancelPrintJob method has been removed from the public crosapi::mojom::LocalPrinter interface. Clients must now use the Ash-specific ash::printing::print_management::PrintingManager API directly. (aca3120)
    ChromeOSPrinting
  • ⚠️The public variations::VariationsServiceClient interface was refactored. Two virtual methods were removed and replaced by a new GetAllProfilesKeys virtual method, requiring embedders to update their implementations. (07c8aa0)
    BrowserInfra
  • ⚠️The Chromecast enable_audio_capture_service build flag and its entire associated subsystem, including the CastAudioInputStream class, have been deleted. Similarly, the enable_perfetto_tracing flag and its perfetto_tracing_client were also removed. (64cab58) (58ad4ce)
    BuildMediaPerformance

Security

  • A vulnerability in Android WebView was fixed where a compromised renderer could bypass the setAcceptThirdPartyCookies(false) policy. The browser process now correctly determines the site_for_cookies. (672e778)
    AndroidSecurityNetwork
  • A potential Site Isolation bypass was fixed. MHTML subframes are now forced to commit with opaque origins to prevent a compromised renderer from spoofing a subframe's origin. (e4b92a6)
    SecurityBlink
  • The Windows updater was hardened with stricter temporary directory selection, sanitized path validation to block directory traversal, and improved bounds controls on the COM RPC layer. (58d2139)
    UpdaterSecurityWindows
  • To prevent data corruption from a compromised renderer, the browser process will now terminate a renderer that supplies invalid index IDs or keys to IndexedDB. (206286e)
    StorageSecurityBlink
  • On ChromeOS, user-imported ONC files can no longer silently establish web trust for root CAs, as TrustBits are now stripped from such configurations. (c0dd46e) (1db6c27)
    ChromeOSSecurityPolicy

Blink & Web Platform

  • The text-fit CSS property has been enabled by default and is now considered stable. Follow-up commits fixed related bugs with line height, text emphasis, and underline offset scaling. (5294214) (98c96b6) (9de481f) (37463c7)
    BlinkCSS
  • The chain value for the overscroll-behavior CSS property has been shipped, allowing web developers to explicitly control scroll chaining behavior. (424d2b9)
    BlinkCSS
  • Initial infrastructure for the Declarative Performance Observer feature was landed, including a header parser and a skeleton PageLoadMetricsObserver. (2d4c09d) (de01842)
    PerformanceBlink
  • A race condition that could lead to premature Service Worker termination has been fixed by introducing a keepalive sequence number. (d92cc43)
    ServiceWorkerBlink
  • To enhance privacy, active sensor connections are now severed immediately when a user revokes the "Sensors" permission for a frame, preventing further data leakage. (19452b0)
    PrivacyBlinkSecurity

Android

  • A bug causing an Application Not Responding (ANR) error when reading archived tab groups was fixed by optimizing the count retrieval logic. (832219f)
    AndroidUITabs
  • A fix was implemented to ensure WebXR sessions always render in fullscreen, addressing a UI "squishing" issue caused by system insets on newer Android versions. (399a6d7)
    XRAndroid
  • The ExtensionURLLoaderThrottle is now correctly enabled on desktop Android, and availability for "controlled frame" APIs has been properly configured for the platform. (accec1e) (46c775e)
    ExtensionsAndroid

ChromeOS & Ash

  • The ongoing effort to decouple Ash from the browser continues, with DateTimeSection, InternetHandler, FingerprintHandler, and FilesSection all migrated to use lower-level Ash APIs instead of ProfileHelper. (4d70581) (a264ff9) (47991d8) (3d2cde3)
    AshChromeOSUI
  • As part of the "GlowUp" UI refresh, a large number of vector icons in ash/ and components/ were renamed to prepare for new icon assets. (c75b4a7) (4a3af8f)
    AshUIThemes
  • In the Camera App (CCA), the cloud save warning dialog was migrated from Polymer's cr-elements to Lit to prevent touch event interception issues. (4918888)
    ChromeOSUI

iOS

  • Significant refactoring unified Gemini/AI entry points, routing the app bar's "Ask" button and the Page Action Menu through a generalized coordinator. (41840d4) (689f458) (c38cd1f)
    iOSUIAI
  • A long-press context menu was implemented for the Tab Grid button on iPad, which now also includes a tab count label. (634615e) (61e469f)
    iOSUITabs
  • The WebAuthnCredentialsDelegate was integrated into the manual fill mediator to enable fetching and displaying passkeys in the manual fallback UI. (e22b581)
    iOSPasswordManagerSecurity
  • Async trace events were added to track the duration of JavaScript execution tasks dispatched to WebKit, aiding in performance analysis on iOS. (a30c87f)
    iOSDevToolsPerformance

Build System & Infrastructure

  • Support was added for Visual Studio 2026, and several third-party libraries were rolled, including libvpx, libaom, ICU, and Rust crates. (1550583) (516d005) (85e3a1d) (36157b4)
    BuildInfra
  • Several obsolete feature flags that have been enabled by default were removed, including kPrefetchFixHeaderUpdatesOnRedirect, kFastPairHandshakeLongTermRefactor, and SubframeImportance. (5b9e0a9) (9eb4f10) (f883619)
    InfraBuild
  • The BackgroundTracingManager was moved from content/ to services/ to allow its use in environments without the full content layer, such as on iOS. (0be4281)
    PerformanceInfra

Performance & Memory

  • The migration from base::MemoryPressureListener to the more flexible base::MemoryConsumer API continues, with BlobMemoryController and SlopBucket being updated to use the new system. (cd4a600) (59d3eb3) (3eec1c7)
    PerformanceBase
  • The concepts of DeviceScaleFactor and RenderedDeviceScaleFactor in LayerTreeHostImpl were unified, simplifying scale factor handling in the compositor. (c923203)
    CompositorPerformance

2026-05-13

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-13

Overview

On May 13, 2026, the Chromium main branch saw 682 commits, with 499 identified as relevant to developers. This summary highlights major API changes, security enhancements, and performance improvements. The day's activity started with commit 5b6de9caa4cd00624e25a4dea1a7adcf9eea2e70 and concluded with 010fba64a502640c492a2d53d47613cbe5881ae6.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The <webview> tag and chrome.webviewTag API have been removed from Chrome Apps on Windows, Mac, and Linux. This feature is now restricted to ChromeOS only, breaking a stable and widely-used API on desktop platforms. (dfa28c4)
    ExtensionsBrowser
  • ⚠️Several PartitionAlloc headers have been moved into an internal/ subdirectory. This is a public API change that will break any build that directly includes headers like partition_page.h or partition_root.h from their old location. (cd836a8)
    BaseBuild
  • ⚠️The core compositor class cc::LayerTreeMutatorClient has been renamed to cc::LayerTreeMutatorDelegate, and its SetClient method is now SetDelegate. This will break embedders who implement or use this animation interface. (34492d7)
    CompositorBlink
  • ⚠️The public SendTabToSelfModelObserver C++ interface has been significantly changed. All method names now require an On prefix (e.g., EntriesAddedRemotely is now OnEntriesAddedRemotely), and the OnSendTabToSelfModelLoaded method has been removed entirely, breaking all existing observer implementations. (35d38a6) (2ca3334)
    SyncBrowser
  • ⚠️The remoting::AudioInjector C++ interface was refactored. It no longer inherits from protocol::AudioStub, and the InjectAudioPacket() method was replaced by a new pure virtual method SetSampleInfo(). Custom audio injection implementations will need to be updated. (5259bac)
    MediaWebRTC
  • ⚠️On Android, the public Java interface ChromeAndroidTaskFeature has changed. The onAddedToTask(long) method was removed and onAddedToTask(InitInfo) is now a mandatory method, which will cause compilation errors for any class implementing this interface. (5af4b45)
    Android
  • ⚠️The PasswordStoreBackend::InitBackend method signature has been changed, removing the AffiliatedMatchHelper* parameter. This will break any custom PasswordStoreBackend implementations. (4fdbebe)
    PasswordManagerStorage
  • ⚠️The public class AccessibilityAnnotationSyncBridge and its usages within SyncServiceFactory have been completely removed. Embedders using this sync data type will be broken. (bc57ea3)
    SyncAccessibility
  • ⚠️On ChromeOS, the GetPolicies method has been removed from the crosapi::mojom::LocalPrinter interface, breaking clients that use it to fetch printing policies. (28102da)
    ChromeOSPrinting

Security & Privacy

  • The PartitionAllocWithAdvancedChecks (MiracleObject) memory safety feature has been enabled by default for the Browser UI process, helping to detect and prevent Use-After-Free vulnerabilities. (34c2ad4)
    SecurityPerformanceBase
  • A site isolation bypass in the Web Serial API for non-default StoragePartitions (e.g., in guest views) has been fixed by blocking access for HTTP/HTTPS origins in those partitions. (16e3c60)
    SecurityBlinkStorage
  • The Windows network service sandbox is now more robust. It brokers all connections to non-publicly-routable addresses to prevent the OS firewall from blocking legitimate local network traffic from sources like VPNs or virtual machine NICs. (3129fd2)
    SecurityWindowsNetwork
  • A privacy leak where cross-origin images inside SVG <mask> elements could be rendered to a <canvas> without tainting it has been fixed. (4855238)
    BlinkSecurityGPU
  • A Use-After-Free crash in DeepScanningRequest has been fixed. The fix prevents synchronous cancellation of a scan request from prematurely deleting the request object while it is still in use. (64a9675)
    SecurityDownloads

Web Platform & Performance

  • The async clipboard read() and readText() APIs were updated to use non-blocking Mojo IPCs in the renderer. This prevents the browser UI from freezing when pasting content from applications that are slow to render it. (a274f36)
    BlinkPerformanceUI
  • The border shorthand property was fixed to correctly reset border-image to its initial value, aligning with CSS specifications and improving web compatibility. (3318b59)
    BlinkCSS
  • Layout performance has been improved by caching SkStrike objects per font, reducing expensive digest computations. This provides a notable benefit for pages with complex text, especially CJK characters. (51e12ce)
    BlinkPerformanceFonts
  • The CSS shape-outside property now supports interpolation when both the start and end shapes share the same <shape-box>, enabling smoother animations and transitions. (11370e4)
    BlinkCSS
  • A renderer crash in the File System Access API has been fixed. The crash occurred when writing a WebAssembly-backed buffer larger than INT_MAX bytes to a SyncAccessHandle. (8965c32)
    BlinkStorageWebAssembly

Android & iOS

  • ⚠️On Android, the TabGroupModelFilterObserver::didCreateNewGroup method signature was changed from (Tab, TabGroupModelFilter) to (Tab, TabModel). Embedders using this observer will need to update their implementations. (2c5f60a)
    AndroidTabs
  • ⚠️On Android, the org.chromium.base.ObserverList Java class now restricts direct access to its mObservers field, marking it @VisibleForTesting. This breaks any production code that was directly accessing this list. (dc6bac0)
    AndroidBase
  • On Android, Chrome will now terminate its own process upon a system locale change. This is a proactive measure to prevent crashes from locale mismatches on the next startup. (1e3c007)
    Android
  • ⚠️On iOS, the public OverlayUIDidFinishDismissal method in the OverlayRequestCoordinatorDelegate interface now passes a safe OverlayRequestId instead of a raw OverlayRequest*, breaking implementations but preventing a class of Use-After-Free bugs. (c3eb345)
    iOSUI

Desktop & Core Internals

  • AppArmor profiles are now included for all Chrome/Chromium Linux installer packages (.deb, .rpm). This fixes sandbox and crash reporting issues on newer Linux distributions like Ubuntu 24.04. (d4c2b79)
    InfraLinuxSecurity
  • The public C++ API on FormFieldData for the experimental Email Verification feature has been changed. The nonce()/set_nonce() methods have been renamed to challenge()/set_challenge(). (0846112)
    AutofillBlink
  • The app_shell binary and its related code, such as media_capture_util, have been fully deprecated and removed from the extensions system. (e2a0758) (937f006)
    ExtensionsBuild

2026-05-12

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-12

Overview

This summary covers 729 commits on the main branch, with 551 deemed relevant to developers. The changes span from (fb302b7) to (4f91821) and include a large number of breaking API changes across C++, Java, and Mojo interfaces.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public autofill::PaymentsAutofillClient interface now requires implementers to add ShowOmniboxAutofillChip() and HideOmniboxAutofillChip(), breaking all downstream classes until the new pure virtual methods are implemented. (e81f974) (240d9ee)
    AutofillPaymentsUI
  • ⚠️The signature for cc::PropertyTree::Node() has changed from returning a pointer (T*) to a const reference (const T&). Callers can no longer check for nullptr and must ensure they use a valid ID. (56af65f)
    CompositorBlink
  • ⚠️Multiple public Java interfaces for Android menus and UI have had their signatures or property models changed, breaking existing implementations. This includes MenuOrKeyboardActionController, FlyoutHandler, and AppMenuItemWithSubmenuProperties. (a30de76) (e591ca0) (6548bf8)
    AndroidUI
  • ⚠️The public remoting::DesktopEnvironment interface was refactored, removing CreateAudioInjector() and TakeAudioWriter() in favor of a new CreateAudioInjector(std::unique_ptr<IpcFifoBufferReader> reader) method. (d4f0d39)
    ServicesMedia
  • ⚠️The public net::ConnectionChangeNotifier::Observer and its Mojo counterpart now require a bool was_ever_used_to_create_streams parameter in the OnSessionClosed method. (7034e9a)
    NetworkServices
  • ⚠️The public Java interface content_public.browser.ImeAdapter has a new required method, setAllowFullscreenIme, which will cause build failures for embedders who implement this interface. (afa6abe)
    AndroidUI
  • ⚠️The signature of the public function chrome::NewSplitTab in chrome/browser/ui/browser_commands.h now requires a split_tabs::SplitTabLayout parameter. (72e8f4b)
    UITabs
  • ⚠️The behavior of the Navigation API has changed. Committed cross-document navigations now clear the 'ongoing navigate event', which may prevent navigateerror events from firing in scenarios like BFCache restores. (0d616ef)
    BlinkNavigation
  • ⚠️The rendering of the legacy CSS property display:-webkit-box now blockifies its children to align with Flexbox behavior, which may break layouts on older websites. (3d82832)
    BlinkCSS
  • ⚠️Public APIs related to WebUI tab dragging in Ash, including ash::ShellDelegate::IsTabDrag and the ash::TabDragDropDelegate class, have been removed. (9db545d)
    AshUI
  • ⚠️The AutofillClient::EntityImportPromptResultCallback signature has changed to include a new base::optional_ref<const EntityInstance> parameter, requiring updates to all implementations. (f2df905)
    AutofillAI
  • ⚠️The AuthenticatorListObserver and ObservableAuthenticatorList classes have been completely removed from chrome/browser/webauthn/, breaking any custom UI integrations that used them. (68f4910)
    SecurityWebAuthn
  • ⚠️The GeolocationContext::BindGeolocation mojom API signature has changed from requiring a const GURL& to a const url::Origin&. (40d7259)
    ServicesPrivacy
  • ⚠️The public accessor tab_contextualization_controller() has been removed from tabs::TabFeatures. Callers must now use the UnownedUserData pattern to access the controller. (5aceda8)
    TabsUI
  • ⚠️The CreatePrintJob method has been removed from the crosapi::mojom::LocalPrinter Mojo interface. (0847328)
    ChromeOSPrinting
  • ⚠️The chromecast::AlarmManager utility class has been deleted from chromecast/base. (9b358a4)
    MediaBase
  • ⚠️A compatibility shim for deprecated JNI Zero APIs was removed from third_party/jni_zero/java_refs.h, which will cause build failures for any remaining users. (56721f4)
    AndroidBuild
  • ⚠️The kLocalNetworkAccess request type has been removed from the public permissions and content_settings APIs. (58ff1a8)
    SecurityPermissions
  • ⚠️Cronet's AndroidProxyOptions API no longer accepts a nullable ProxyOptions object or an empty proxy list. (ae18535) (da306c9)
    NetworkAndroid

Web Platform & Blink

  • The CSS :overscroll-open pseudo-class has been implemented to track when an overscroll area is active. The overscroll target is now also inert when not open. (7fa4ec3) (b21653a)
    BlinkCSS
  • A Service Worker interceptor has been added to the download manager, allowing service workers to handle downloads initiated from browser UI actions like "Save As". (a2fbe85)
    ServiceWorkerDownloads
  • The Extensions alarms API now includes a persistAcrossSessions flag to control whether alarms persist across browser restarts, aligning with a WECG proposal. (900c2b0)
    Extensions
  • Support for the CSS image(<color>) functional notation from CSS Images Level 4 has been implemented behind a feature flag. (8a31b8a)
    BlinkCSS
  • IntersectionObserver occlusion detection has been improved to better handle elements with 3D transforms, reducing false positives. (bd82770)
    BlinkPerformance
  • The behavior of scrollIntoView has been updated to better align with scroll-snap-align per a CSSWG resolution. (1c908f0)
    BlinkCSS

Services & Security

  • A security fix was implemented in MediaFoundationVideoEncodeAccelerator to add validity checks and prevent out-of-bounds access on D3D11 textures from untrusted visible_rect data. (cce48a3)
    SecurityMediaWindows
  • For improved privacy, Glic (Gemini in Chrome) screenshots now ensure sensitive fields in out-of-process iframes are redacted, even if the main frame attempts to omit them. (f588883)
    PrivacySecurityAI
  • A user setting has been implemented to allow overriding the navigator.cpuPerformance tier, and helper functions for CPU info have been exposed via the public content API. (2e3a831)
    PerformanceServices
  • Support for successful HTTP/3 WebSocket handshakes has been added, which now use a 200 response code similar to HTTP/2. (d8afde9)
    Network
  • WebAuthn enclave websockets are now given maximum network priority to improve the reliability of passkey requests. (5239846)
    WebAuthnNetworkSecurity

Android

  • A security fix was implemented in the Android WebShare API to enforce SafeBaseName validations, preventing potential path traversal attacks. (a6afd71)
    AndroidSecurity
  • The Side Panel's behavior during window resizing was improved to automatically close and reopen the panel while respecting minimum content widths. (7f87194) (bc24808)
    AndroidUI
  • The Glic (Gemini in Chrome) button on the tab strip received new animations, and its settings are now synced with the tab strip pin preference. (94e38ab) (693b542)
    AndroidUIAI

GPU & Compositor

  • A new compositor-driven BeginFrameSource implementation for Wayland was introduced behind the kWaylandExternalBeginFrameSource flag, aiming for more accurate and efficient frame pacing. (fb302b7)
    GPUCompositorLinux
  • A bug in Canvas's getElementTransform was fixed to correctly preserve the 3D/2D nature of matrices, preventing incorrect scaling for WebGL and WebGPU content. (0380f5f)
    BlinkWebGLWebGPU

Feature & Code Cleanup

  • Numerous expired feature flags have been removed from the codebase, including CCTToolbarRefactor, LockTopControlsOnTablet, EditEmojiTagSequence, AndroidAutofillForwardIframeOrigin, and kWebViewWebauthn. (d21c401) (d9fa702) (2a22940) (91191a9) (6135710)
    AndroidAutofillWebAuthn
  • The Read Anything accessibility feature was updated to better map distilled content to the accessibility tree, enabling text selection within the simplified view. (29ecc38)
    AccessibilityUI
  • The legacy Google "Super G" vector icon has been removed and replaced with the newer gradient icon in all contexts. (9e28f43)
    UIThemes
  • A significant refactoring effort is underway in ChromeOS to improve OS/browser isolation by moving preferences from chrome/common to ash/constants. (b075bf4) (5827aaf) (dded95f)
    ChromeOSAsh

2026-05-11

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-11

Overview

This daily summary for the Chromium main branch on May 11, 2026, covers 596 commits, with 456 identified as relevant to developers. The changes span from the first commit b458747572a9 to the last 6ab45a706ac3 and include a significant number of breaking changes across the build system, networking, and various public C++ and Mojom APIs.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The monolithic //chrome/browser build target has been broken into many smaller source_set targets. Embedders must update their BUILD.gn files to depend on the new, more specific targets instead of the old umbrella target. (a235e83)
    BuildBrowser
  • ⚠️Multiple core networking functions in net/socket and net/http have been changed to accept a target_network parameter. This is a widespread public API change affecting ConnectJobFactory::CreateConnectJob, HttpProxySocketParams, and others. (6f90916)
    Network
  • ⚠️Two new pure virtual methods, SurfaceEmbedChildWebContentsAttached and SurfaceEmbedChildWebContentsDetached, have been added to the public observer interface content::WebContentsObserver. Subclasses must be updated. (4a7f0a4)
    UIPerformance
  • ⚠️A new pure virtual function GetWebContentsFromWindowIfCaptureHandleAllowed has been added to the public interface content::ContentBrowserClient. Embedders subclassing this client must implement the new method. (3221e4a)
    MediaUISecurity
  • ⚠️The public media::AudioHandler interface now includes a new pure virtual function, CopyPartialFramesTo. All subclasses must implement this function to compile. (67d7e23)
    Media
  • ⚠️The signature of the virtual method MaybeReportPasswordReuseDetected in the public class PasswordProtectionServiceBase has changed to include a const ReferrerChain& parameter. Overriding subclasses will fail to compile. (3b5907c)
    PasswordManagerSecurityEnterprise
  • ⚠️The string representation of the public API content::DesktopMediaID has changed to support native picker sessions. Code that manually parses or generates these ID strings will break. (045d84e)
    MediaWebRTC
  • ⚠️The signature of the public mojom interface device::mojom::XRFrameDataProvider::SubmitFrameDrawnIntoTexture has changed to include a new camera_sync_tokens parameter. (02a16b8)
    XRMedia
  • ⚠️The return type of the public Web Crypto API SubtleCrypto.verify() has been changed from Promise<any> to Promise<boolean> to align with the specification. Web applications relying on the previous non-standard behavior may break. (14ff7bb)
    BlinkCryptoSecurity
  • ⚠️The long-deprecated app_shell binary and its associated sources have been removed from extensions/shell. Any remaining build dependencies on this target will break. (75cd2c6)
    ExtensionsBuild
  • ⚠️The signature of the public mojom interface network::mojom::RestrictedCookieManager::SetCookieFromString has changed, removing the get_version_shared_memory parameter and CookiesResponse return value. (05514ce)
    NetworkPrivacy
  • ⚠️Media parsing has been made stricter to reject MP4 files with duplicate track IDs, per specification. Previously playable but non-compliant files will now fail to load. (63afd1b)
    MediaBlink
  • ⚠️The GetOemData method has been removed from the crosapi.mojom.TelemetryProbeService interface. Clients on ChromeOS must update to use DebugDaemonClient::GetLog instead. (c89c1be)
    ChromeOS
  • ⚠️The public constructor for TextBubble.Builder on Android has been changed, removing the isAccessibilityEnabled parameter. Callers must update to use the new signature. (121ecaa)
    AndroidUI
  • ⚠️The signature of the virtual method ContentSettingsProvider::SetWebsiteSetting has been changed from taking base::Value&& to const base::Value&. Custom provider implementations must be updated. (41de479)
    PrivacyBlink
  • ⚠️The type of the public member encoded_body_size in net::HttpResponseInfo has been changed from std::optional<int64_t> to std::optional<base::ByteSize>, which is a source and binary breaking change. (ec45984)
    Network

Security

  • A TOCTOU vulnerability in the WebUSB API was fixed by locking interface requests during pending device state changes. (7e97805)
    SecurityUSBBlink
  • Input event routing was hardened by adding validation for the FrameSinkId in async hit-test responses, preventing a compromised renderer from redirecting input to arbitrary cross-origin frames. (1bdeadd)
    SecurityUICompositor
  • A use-after-unmap vulnerability in the Web Serial API on Windows was resolved by ensuring data pipe handles are kept alive until all pending I/O completes. (c1ee3a3)
    SecurityBlinkWindows
  • On-demand spell check requests are now restricted to run only after explicit user interactions with a focused element, mitigating potential user dictionary leak attacks. (46881f7)
    SecurityUIPrivacy
  • A vulnerability was fixed where GuestViews could bypass WebHID enterprise policies, allowing a malicious host to hijack HID access from an embedded origin. (63d25d3)
    SecurityExtensions
  • A potential information leak in the Gamepad API, where browser stack data could be copied into shared memory, has been fixed. (f7b517d)
    SecurityBlinkMedia

Build & Infrastructure

  • The enable_swift_cxx_interop flag was removed from iOS build configurations as the feature is no longer being actively pursued. (1ad2be3)
    BuildiOS
  • A change to roll out the faster Apple linker for non-official macOS builds was landed and then reverted due to build breakages. (a086c9b) (df36205)
    BuildmacOS
  • Dependencies for the libafl fuzzer were added for testing purposes. (03b7245) (f219890) (e8fac8e)
    BuildTestingSecurity

Networking

  • A 'maximum priority' mode was added for WebSockets, intended for internal browser use cases, which uses MAXIMUM_PRIORITY and LOAD_IGNORE_LIMITS flags. (d09d6a6)
    Network
  • A potential crash in SpdyStream was resolved by deferring stream destruction during a session drain, changing an internal synchronous operation to asynchronous. (eb721a8)
    Network
  • A bug fix in SSPI authentication on Windows restricts delegation flags to the Negotiate scheme, preventing a crash in NTLM authentication. (0996e94)
    NetworkWindowsSecurity

Blink & Rendering

  • Support was added for ruby-overhang: spaces, allowing ruby annotations to overhang adjacent whitespace. (d935f57)
    BlinkCSS
  • The paint implementation for text-decoration-skip-spaces was added. (7c50cb6)
    BlinkCSS
  • A crash was fixed in typed arithmetic calc() expressions involving multiplication of non-number types. (57c87d4)
    BlinkCSS
  • ⚠️The cc::LayerTreeHost::SetNeedsAnimate signature was reverted. This is a breaking change for any code that had adapted to the new signature introduced in a very recent commit. (c6623ae)
    CompositorPerformance

Performance & Memory

  • The compositor's ResourcePool will now discard all resources when the viewport is resized, significantly reducing memory spikes during window resizing. (d6a5cb9)
    PerformanceCompositor
  • A new base::MultiMemoryConsumer class was introduced to allow a single class to manage multiple granular memory interventions, as part of a migration away from MemoryPressureListener. (a5ea88b) (61dfb28)
    PerformanceBase
  • ⚠️The non_main_world_entries() method was removed from the public class V8DetailedMemoryProcessData via a revert, breaking any code that had adopted the new API. (4c257ac)
    V8Performance

Password Manager & Autofill

  • Multiple components, including SavedPasswordsPresenter and PasswordReuseDetector, were refactored to use the new StoredCredential struct instead of the legacy PasswordForm. (25838de) (e32cf6b)
    PasswordManagerAutofill
  • Several expired WebAuthn feature flags, including kWebAuthnSignalApiHidePasskeys and kPasskeyUnlockManager, were cleaned up from the codebase. (8c48777) (89f764e) (1f4bde8)
    PasswordManagerBlink

Accessibility (A11y)

  • A major internal refactoring on Windows removed custom ATL dependencies from ui/accessibility, replacing them with modern WRL and hand-written COM implementations. Public COM interface compatibility should be unaffected. (54afab9)
    AccessibilityWindows
  • Strict validation was added to AXTree updates to reject invalid node IDs, preventing potential crashes and tree corruption. (50d789f)
    AccessibilityBlink

Feature Removals & Reverts

  • A behavioral change that made the bookmark bar visible on custom New Tab Pages was reverted due to user feedback. (01e71df)
    UIBookmarks
  • A change removing Mac power observers for CADisplayLink was reverted as it caused unresponsiveness after sleep/wake cycles. (3dc3598)
    macOSPerformanceCompositor
  • The kClientSideDetectionDeprecateDOMModel feature flag and related code for an old client-side phishing detection model have been removed. (38d0d2f)
    SecurityPrivacy

2026-05-10

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-10

Overview

This digest covers 11 commits from the Chromium project. Key highlights include the default enablement of the TreesInViz architecture, ongoing refactoring of the Android Custom Tabs toolbar, and various internal improvements to UI features, font rendering, and build configurations. No major breaking changes to stable public APIs were identified in this set of commits.

Compositor & Graphics

  • The TreesInViz feature, which moves more layer tree management into the Viz process, is now enabled by default on most platforms (excluding Linux). A subsequent fix was added to correctly propagate screenshot_destination_token in this mode, resolving timeouts in screenshot-based tests. CI bot configurations were also updated to reflect the new default. (b6929d0) (0099a3c) (aeba8a1)
    CompositorGPUInfra

Android

  • As part of an ongoing cleanup, the legacy implementation path for the Custom Chrome Tabs (CCT) toolbar has been removed. The modern, refactored toolbar implementation (CCTToolbarRefactor) is now used unconditionally, simplifying the codebase and removing obsolete metrics. (e6f2c6b)
    AndroidUI

UI & Features

  • A new feature parameter makes the tab glow hover delay configurable, allowing for UX experimentation without requiring code changes. (01b3460)
    UITabsPerformance
  • The TabContextualizationController is now accessed using the UserData-driven (UUD) pattern, a code health refactoring with no change in behavior. (78c3487)
    UITabs
  • A new metric, Glic.Onboarding.OptInImpression, has been added via the Glic mojo API to track impressions of a user onboarding screen. (aa9c8cb)
    UIPrivacy

Internals & Refactoring

  • In the font rendering engine, UNSAFE_TODO()s in OpenType CPAL parsing were eliminated by switching from std::unique_ptr<T[]> to the memory-safe base::HeapArray<T>. (d2bba9e)
    BlinkFontsSecurity
  • Build system configurations for protobuf were improved by adding a proper proto_library target for duration.proto, removing a build hack and ensuring correct path resolution. (f3144dd)
    BuildInfra
  • A test-only helper function, chrome::IsOffTheRecordBrowserInUse(), was inlined at its call sites as a preliminary step toward its eventual removal. (f141690)
    TestingBrowser
  • Automated commit to update translation files (.xtb) across various product components based on the latest source strings. (128654c)
    I18n

2026-05-09

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-09

Overview

This digest covers 57 commits from May 9, 2026, from commit (e8f4b02) to (37bfc56). Four major breaking changes were identified in core dependencies, UI element tracking, extensions, and security handling. Other key updates include the introduction of a new MIME handler fallback API, AI and personal context API development, and numerous bug fixes and refactors across Android, Ash, and WebUI components.

Infrastructure & Build

  • ⚠️A dependency roll of libc++ removes Chromium's custom versions of standard C headers, including fenv.h, ctype.h, and inttypes.h. This is a core dependency update that may cause build failures for embedders who were implicitly or explicitly relying on definitions from the now-removed custom headers. (abbeaf7)
    BuildInfra
  • The shared public agents repository has been added as a new dependency in //agents/shared to contain shareable agentic configurations. Additionally, unused Python utilities for chrome_proxy were removed, and various OWNERS files and documentation were updated. (e754fe8) (6a4b6f7) (029c2f1) (0e193a9) (954bfad)
    InfraAIBuild

Security

  • ⚠️As a security hardening measure, renderers that incorrectly provide an Origin header on navigation requests will now be terminated. This replaces the previous, non-fatal DumpWithoutCrashing behavior and may cause previously stable renderers with this bug to crash unexpectedly. (87ecad0)
    SecurityNetwork
  • Several site isolation feature flags, including enable-site-isolation-for-password-sites and isolate-origins, have been marked as never-expiring to ensure their continued availability for debugging and user opt-in scenarios. (2ed859d)
    SecurityPrivacy

Extensions

  • ⚠️The Web Store component hosted app is no longer loaded on non-ChromeOS platforms (e.g., Windows, Mac, Linux). This removes a long-standing component that was an implementation detail for the old webstore origin. This will break any automation, tests, or embedder features that relied on its presence. (22c9e8a)
    ExtensionsChromeOS
  • A new API, chrome.mimeHandler.abortAndFallbackToNativeHandler(), has been introduced. This allows third-party MIME handler extensions to surrender a response back to the browser's native handler, preventing the extension from being locked into rendering an error for unsupported content subtypes. (d39ac48)
    ExtensionsPDF

UI & User Education

  • ⚠️The ui::TrackedElementWebUI API for anchoring UI to WebUI content has been refactored. Tracked elements are now considered invisible when their containing WebContents is hidden. This behavioral change to a core UI framework component will require consumers to adapt to the new visibility rules. (c00f0b9)
    UI
  • The Omnibox UI is being refactored with the implementation of the OmniboxPopupFullPresenter class. In a separate change, files uploaded from Google Drive via the Omnibox context menu are now added as context to the searchbox. (e8f4b02) (6437bfc)
    UIAI
  • On Windows, Stylus Handwriting support has been extended to function correctly when the text input element is within a cross-process iframe (OOPIF). (d09496a)
    UIWindowsInput

Android

  • Several UI bug fixes and improvements were made, including resolving a memory leak in ToolbarProgressBar, fixing Side Panel visibility restoration across window resizes, and refactoring the Tab Bottom Sheet's WebView resizing to prevent visual artifacts during animations. (7552f56) (52daeae) (eac15fc)
    AndroidUI
  • The PDF toolbar is now inflated using a ViewStub, deferring its creation until it is actually needed. This improves performance by avoiding unnecessary layout inflation on PDF load. (2f29a3e)
    AndroidPDFPerformance

AI & Personalization

  • A client-side API skeleton for the Context Memory Service (CMS) has been defined. This introduces the FetchContext method to the PersonalContextService interface, allowing clients to retrieve context from the service. (adc0707)
    AIPrivacy
  • A comprehensive test harness, including schemas and a runner script, has been added for the MAGI multi-agent protocol to prevent regressions in prompt instructions and state machine transitions. (375df35) (962cb01)
    AITesting
  • On iOS, a bug was fixed that caused a Gemini session to start automatically when the Page Action Menu was opened on a managed account, rather than waiting for user intent. (a9c08a4)
    iOSAI

Ash & ChromeOS

  • A series of tests for network UI components, including NetworkListMobileHeaderViewTest and WifiToggleNotificationControllerTest, were modernized to use targeted synchronization APIs instead of the less reliable RunUntilIdle() method. (d1d4fe9) (77e4fae) (34ac26f) (1c261d6)
    AshTestingNetwork
  • A dangling pointer in ash::Shell has been fixed, and other minor refactors were performed to improve code health. (34f46e9) (295ca52)
    Ash

Web Platform

  • A clock drift bug in WebrtcAudioModule that caused an audible cracking sound has been fixed. The module now adjusts the number of audio frames it pulls based on the actual time elapsed since the last poll, ensuring synchronization with the audio sink. (29e7f53)
    WebRTCMedia
  • WebGL behavior is now more spec-compliant: ETC/ETC1 compressed texture formats are disallowed in texStorage calls until the corresponding WEBGL_compressed_texture_etc or WEBGL_compressed_texture_etc1 extension is explicitly enabled by the application. (6e73bdc)
    WebGLGPU
  • A bug fix in Blink's rendering engine ensures that backgrounds paint correctly up to the border-box origin when the experimental border-shape CSS property is used, as the previous optimization incorrectly contracted the background area. (b1b03e1)
    BlinkCSS
  • The PDFium engine was refactored to prevent potential dangling pointers by deferring the deletion of page objects after they are removed from a page, allowing for safe reloading of text data before the objects are freed. (34fa380)
    PDF

2026-05-08

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-08

Overview

This daily summary covers 677 commits to the Chromium main branch on May 8, 2026, with 496 commits filtered as relevant to developers. The day was marked by an exceptionally high number of breaking API changes across core components like Content, UI, and Android JNI, requiring significant updates for embedders. Notable feature work includes enabling focusgroup by default and hardening security around file pickers. The first relevant commit was fa57518e6fa156ae52dc1ced184692a457706a4d and the last was d06297d18862dbe4e2d48633ec210c8befc5ade2.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public method SiteInstance::GetSiteURL() has been removed from the core content API. Embedders must update their code to use SiteInstance::GetSecurityPrincipal().GetDeprecatedSiteURL() instead. (01d357d)
    ContentSecurity
  • ⚠️A new pure virtual function, CouldDisplayBeforeUnloadDialog(), has been added to the public content::RenderFrameHost interface. Embedders who subclass RenderFrameHost must implement this new method. (8229aec)
    ContentSecurity
  • ⚠️The signature of the public virtual method WebLocalFrameClient::DidFinishSameDocumentNavigation has been changed to include a new bool caused_by_ad parameter. Embedders must update their implementations. (30a999b)
    BlinkContent
  • ⚠️The public Java JNI contract for Android has been broken in two ways: support for the ("module_name") parameter in the @NativeMethods annotation has been removed, and several public functions in jni_zero/java_refs.h were renamed to fix a typo (e.g., IsConvertableToJavaObject is now IsConvertibleToJavaObject). (8567d0e) (585e377)
    AndroidBuild
  • ⚠️The legacy IPC param traits systems in cc/ipc and ui/latency/ipc have been removed as part of the migration to Mojom. Embedders relying on these for compositor or latency IPC will be broken. (b9c0916) (6942ef8)
    CompositorUI
  • ⚠️The public sessions::LiveTab interface now has a new virtual GetSessionID() method. Custom implementations of this interface must add this method. (ef5b7fa)
    BrowserTabs
  • ⚠️The signature of KeyframeModel::SetRunState has been changed, removing the monotonic_time parameter. Subclasses of cc::KeyframeModel or gfx::KeyframeModel must be updated. (2ffd82b)
    CompositorGPU
  • ⚠️The public one_time_tokens component API has been broken. OneTimeTokenService::Subscribe now requires a OneTimeTokenSource parameter, and the OneTimeToken struct now uses base::TimeTicks instead of base::Time. (66bc3eb) (b179842)
    SecurityServices
  • ⚠️On Android, the ContentSettingsType.LOCAL_NETWORK_ACCESS value has been removed from the public enum. Code referencing this enum value will no longer compile. (320801d)
    AndroidNetworkSecurity
  • ⚠️On Android, several deprecated public constructors have been removed from the components.browser_ui.widget.android.TextBubble Java class. (4d616a9)
    AndroidUI
  • ⚠️On ChromeOS, the document_scan.mojom Crosapi interface and its implementation have been completely removed. Clients using this for document scanning will be broken. (46d30e8) (c6ab520)
    ChromeOSAsh
  • ⚠️The public method items() has been removed from the device::HidReportDescriptor class. (6328251)
    USBServices
  • ⚠️Several public HTML attribute accessor methods (e.g., GetHtmlAttribute) have been removed from the core accessibility class ui::accessibility::AXNodeData. (a692855)
    AccessibilityUI
  • ⚠️The shared WebUI file util_deprecated.js has been moved from chrome://resources/js/ to the ChromeOS-specific path chrome://resources/ash/common/, breaking any non-ChromeOS WebUI pages that included it. (cef26d4)
    UIChromeOS

Security

  • The origin of workers created from data: URLs is now correctly set to a new, unique opaque origin per the HTML spec. This is a behavioral change that may break content that relied on the previous non-compliant behavior of inheriting the creator's origin. (ee6bfd1)
    BlinkSecurityServiceWorker
  • The File System Access API has been hardened by restricting cross-origin subframes from showing a file picker. A new ContentBrowserClient method, IsCrossOriginSubframeAllowedToShowFilePicker, allows embedders to grant explicit exemptions. (3fd5e2c)
    SecurityBlinkContent
  • A browser-side check now strictly enforces that a page must have user activation to show a beforeunload dialog. (aa69447)
    SecurityUI
  • On iOS, WebAuthn requests are now correctly restricted to secure (HTTPS) origins. (1dc7714)
    iOSSecurityWebRTC

Web Platform

  • The focusgroup accessibility feature has been enabled by default, graduating from its origin trial and improving keyboard navigation for custom components. (5fdbeaa)
    AccessibilityBlinkHTML
  • Web Bluetooth's startNotifications() method now correctly blocks notifications for characteristics blocklisted with EXCLUDE_READS. This aligns with the spec but is a breaking behavioral change for web applications that relied on the old behavior. (8b64a2a)
    BluetoothBlinkSecurity
  • SVG text elements now honor standard text-decoration CSS properties, as the SvgEnableTextDecorationCssStyling feature has been enabled by default. (ec86856)
    BlinkCSSSVG
  • The ExtensionTabContextMenu feature has been enabled by default, allowing extensions to use the "tab" context in contextMenus.create. (04fa747)
    Extensions
  • A memory leak involving the :not pseudo-class and the subsequent-sibling combinator (~) has been fixed. (08b0075)
    BlinkCSS

Android

  • New JNI APIs have been added to Android WebView to allow embedders to parse request bodies for content restriction purposes. (9fc7a63)
    Android
  • The user agent string for Android Desktop mode can now be spoofed as ChromeOS via the new DesktopModeUaChromeOs feature flag. (8dedf95)
    AndroidUI
  • The URL bar on large form factor devices is now multiline by default to fix text selection bugs. (765dfaa)
    AndroidUI
  • Disabled extensions in the toolbar menu are now correctly exposed to screen readers. (4a80d65)
    AndroidAccessibilityExtensions

Services and Components

  • The V8 memory reporter in Performance Manager will now include memory usage from non-main-world V8 contexts (e.g., extensions), providing more detailed memory data. (7aaf1da)
    PerformanceV8Extensions
  • The Private AI backend is now enabled by default, although individual features that use it remain behind their own feature flags. (98b7d23)
    AIServices
  • A new enterprise policy, GeminiExperimentalTriggeringSettings, was implemented to allow administrators to control experimental Gemini features. (38dcc2f)
    EnterprisePolicyAI

Removals and Cleanup

  • ⚠️The public method ClearLocalCvcsUpToMay2025() has been removed from PaymentsDataManager and its backends, as the temporary cleanup function is no longer needed. (466b250)
    PaymentsAutofill
  • Legacy "v1" feature flags for PWA Navigation Capturing have been removed. (7f05356)
    BlinkPWA
  • The feature flags for kCommittedOriginTracking and kCommittedOriginEnforcements have been removed now that the security features are finalized. (eceba83)
    SecurityContent
  • ⚠️The virtual method Now() has been removed from base::DiscardableSharedMemory and its service-side manager. (46103bb)
    BaseMemory

2026-05-07

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-07

Overview

This digest covers 776 commits on the main branch, with 573 deemed relevant to developers. The day saw a large number of breaking changes across core APIs, including the GPU stack, networking, and multiple public interfaces. Major themes include ongoing security hardening, API modernization by removing legacy patterns, and significant refactoring in the media and graphics pipelines. Changes span from (175b215) to (81c154b).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️Constructors for SharedImageBacking and all its subclasses (e.g., D3DImageBacking, AndroidImageBacking) have been changed to accept a SharedImageInfo struct instead of individual parameters. This is a major signature change affecting embedders who implement or instantiate these core GPU classes. (d8351b2)
    GPU
  • ⚠️On Android, a new pure virtual method SetDrawPaused(bool paused) was added to the public content::Compositor interface. All embedder implementations must be updated to provide this method. (6c313d1)
    AndroidCompositor
  • ⚠️The signature of the core public API content::WebContents::ForSecurityDropFullscreen has changed. The return type is now std::optional<base::ScopedClosureRunner> to prevent a use-after-free bug. Callers must update their code to handle the new optional return type. (058c2d6)
    BrowserSecurity
  • ⚠️Legacy IPC serialization headers content/common/common_param_traits.h and extensions/common/common_param_traits.h have been removed. Embedders relying on these for serializing enums must migrate to the modern mojom system. (bd1a47d) (faa0748)
    BaseNetworkExtensions
  • ⚠️The public compositor interface cc::LayerTreeHostSingleThreadClient has been renamed to cc::LayerTreeHostSingleThreadDelegate. Embedders using single-threaded compositing must implement the new delegate interface. (2b1a6ff)
    Compositor
  • ⚠️The Web Request API's core data structures (WebRequestInfo, WebRequestInfoInitParams) now use the strongly-typed GlobalRenderFrameHostId instead of integer-based process/frame IDs. This breaks embedders and extensions that rely on the previous structure. (0f97ab9)
    ExtensionsNetwork
  • ⚠️The tex_coord_rect field has been removed from CompositorRenderPassDrawQuad and AggregatedRenderPassDrawQuad, and the signatures of their SetNew and SetAll methods have been altered. This breaks embedders who interact directly with compositor frame data. (871a3b5)
    CompositorGPU
  • ⚠️Multiple public methods have been removed from blink::platform::text::TextRun, including operator[], Span8(), Span16(), and Is8Bit(). Code using these methods will no longer compile. (88757c5)
    BlinkFonts
  • ⚠️In the WebAudio module, several public constructors for FFTFrame have been deleted, and overloaded vector math functions in vector_math.h that accepted const float* have been removed. Code relying on these will fail to compile. (efab762) (0bb6180) (d9051d0)
    BlinkMedia
  • ⚠️The Web Feed (Following feed) feature has been removed. This includes the removal of the public RssLinksFetcher API and the feed::mojom::RssLinkReader mojom interface. (19de65f)
    UINetwork
  • ⚠️The DevTools Protocol for CSS Container Queries has been changed. The setContainerQueryText command and text property are deprecated and replaced by setContainerQueryConditionText and conditionText, respectively. (3bc3aaa)
    DevToolsCSS
  • ⚠️The public method net::URLRequest::set_force_ignore_site_for_cookies() has been removed. Embedders using this API will need to find an alternative or remove its usage. (c0edd27)
    NetworkPrivacy
  • ⚠️The origin parameter has been removed from the public Mojo interface blink::mojom::FileSystemManager::Open. Callers must now rely on the browser-authoritative origin. (5c6dc2f)
    BlinkStorage
  • ⚠️A behavioral change to the stable Bookmarks API alters folder visibility logic on desktop Android via BookmarkNode::IsVisible(). A new virtual method GetBookmarkFormFactor() was also added to the bookmarks::BookmarkClient interface, which may require embedder updates. (85f5cc3)
    BookmarksUIAndroid
  • ⚠️The Android printing API has been changed to fix a Use-After-Close vulnerability. The native PrintingContextAndroid now takes ownership of the file descriptor, and the Java JNI method getFileDescriptor has been replaced with takeDuplicatedFileDescriptor. (9c66405)
    AndroidPrintingSecurity

Security

  • Multiple Use-After-Free (UAF) vulnerabilities were addressed. Fixes were landed in RenderWidgetHostViewAura after synchronous IME calls, ChromeDriver's WebAuthn and alert command handlers, and WindowEventDispatcher for gesture events. (a66bf3a) (de99d4d) (77dde5f) (a861deb)
    SecurityUIDevTools
  • Path validation was tightened in several areas to prevent security issues. A path traversal vulnerability in the iOS Download Manager was fixed, and directory traversal was prevented for background image requests in UntrustedSource. (b1e96f2) (e0fd6c0)
    SecurityiOSDownloads
  • A bug in LaunchQueue was fixed to prevent a page from stealing File System Access handles by aborting a launch navigation. (2375aea)
    SecurityStorage

Blink & Web Platform

  • The caret-color CSS property was updated to support an optional second color value for block carets, improving styling options for text cursors. (c50f753) (b02571a)
    BlinkCSS
  • The experimental WebNN API was refactored, moving the Dispatch method from the WebNNGraph to the WebNNContext mojom interface to better preserve message ordering. (0006743)
    BlinkAI
  • ⚠️The StaticRange DOM API implementation was corrected to derive its owner document from its start container, fixing issues with cross-realm range validation. The constructor signature was also changed, constituting a breaking change. (dd75fa5)
    BlinkHTML
  • ⚠️The accessibility event Event::kFocusContext has been functionally removed by unmapping it from platform-specific events. Code that dispatches or listens for this event will no longer work as expected. (95cdd5f) (95388be)
    AccessibilityUI

Platform Specifics

  • On Android, the PDF viewer has been refactored to be loaded on demand as a dynamic module, improving startup performance and reducing initial memory footprint. (bd7f626)
    AndroidPDF
  • The approximate geolocation permission (kApproximateGeolocationPermission) has been enabled on Android, and numerous tests were adapted to account for the new permission state. (96d8c5e) (75d3c15)
    AndroidPrivacy
  • On ChromeOS, Direct Sockets are now enabled for the Terminal app. This is part of a migration away from the older Chrome Sockets APIs. (cba1aaa)
    ChromeOSNetwork
  • ⚠️On macOS, the VideoCaptureProvider public API was changed by renaming GetMainBundleId to GetApplicationAudioCaptureId to better reflect its purpose in the native screen picker. (c9e372a)
    macOSMedia

Build & Infrastructure

  • Component build support has been enabled for Rust crates, improving developer iteration time when working with Rust code in Chromium. (decfd1e)
    BuildInfra
  • ⚠️A new Rust-based XML parser has been introduced. As part of this work, the public API data_decoder::xml::Element::GetFirstChildByTagName() was renamed to FindFirstChildByTagName(). (396076d)
    BaseBuild
  • ⚠️The SoundsManager class has been moved from chromeos/ash/components/audio to the cross-platform services/audio/public/cpp/sounds. This file path change will break builds for embedders including the header from its old location. (608ac09)
    ChromeOSMediaBuild

2026-05-06

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-06

Overview

This summary covers 558 relevant commits out of 741 total from the main branch on May 6th, 2026. This digest highlights numerous breaking changes to core C++ interfaces and web platform APIs, the shipment of new CSS features, and significant security fixes. First commit: (dcf44d8), Last commit: (056c439).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public Android WebView API getAwContents() now blocks and waits for Chromium to initialize. This can cause deadlocks or ANRs in applications that previously called it on the UI thread without expecting it to block. (97e5ca6)
    Android
  • ⚠️The scrollIntoView() JavaScript API has changed its default alignment. The block and inline options now default to "auto" instead of "start" and "nearest", which may alter scrolling behavior on websites relying on the previous defaults. (5972c54)
    BlinkCSS
  • ⚠️The media::AudioManager interface underwent rapid, breaking changes. Methods GetAudioInputDeviceNames and GetAudioOutputDeviceNames were changed from returning void to bool, and then reverted back to void. Embedders must ensure their implementation matches the final void return type. (8ffee52) (470bf4c)
    MediaAudio
  • ⚠️A new pure virtual function, RemoveGoogleGroupsFromPrefsForDeletedProfiles, has been added to the variations::VariationsServiceClient public interface. All embedders must now implement this method to handle profile data cleanup. (8c8d5ff)
    EnterpriseProfiles
  • ⚠️For privacy, the Long Tasks API now redacts detailed attribution (containerId, containerName, containerSrc) for long tasks originating in cross-origin iframes. Performance monitoring tools relying on this data will be affected. (f34f0ba)
    BlinkPerformancePrivacy
  • ⚠️The web-serial-wired-devices-android feature has been removed. This constitutes a removal of a stable, public API for websites that were using the Web Serial API to interact with wired serial devices on Android. (1113e33)
    BlinkAndroidUSB
  • ⚠️The PaymentsAutofillClient interface has been updated, removing several virtual methods including LoadRiskData and GetVirtualCardBenefitDetails. Embedders implementing this interface must update their code. (99e0d54)
    PaymentsAutofill
  • ⚠️The public method set_owned_by_client() has been removed from the foundational ui::views::View class. Code should now manage view ownership directly. (04fbab8)
    UI
  • ⚠️Global functions for managing metrics reporting consent (e.g., ChangeMetricsReportingState) have been moved into the metrics:: namespace, breaking existing callers. (f723259)
    BasePrivacy
  • ⚠️The extensions::MimeTypesHandler::GetHandler() method was renamed to Get() and its signature changed to accept a reference. Additionally, extensions::Extension::GetManifestData() now returns a const pointer. (d1eb114) (9d8833a)
    Extensions
  • ⚠️On iOS, the public method FetchCanSigninToChrome was removed from SystemIdentityManager, and the web::ScriptMessage constructor signature was changed to require a url::Origin. (e11c0f8) (f25be9b)
    iOS

Web Platform & Blink

  • New CSS features have been shipped: URL request modifiers (cross-origin(), referrer-policy(), integrity()) for resources fetched via CSS are now enabled. (e1c6eac)
    BlinkCSS
  • New CSS media pseudo-classes (:playing, :paused, :seeking, :buffering, :stalled, :muted) are now enabled by default, allowing easier styling based on media state. (cefbd1e)
    BlinkCSSMedia
  • The popover=hint attribute, which creates non-modal, auto-dismissing popovers, has been shipped and is now enabled by default. (a4e8e34)
    BlinkHTML
  • To prevent stack overflow crashes from malicious CSS, depth limits are now enforced on flat math expression chains (e.g., 1px * 1px * ...) during parsing. (1a31de5)
    BlinkCSSSecurity
  • For WebAudio, the promise resolution order for setSinkId() has been corrected to fire the onstatechange event after the promise resolves, not before, improving spec compliance. (262fe80)
    BlinkMedia
  • In WebCodecs, H.264 content with a 4:4:4 chroma subsampling profile will now be routed to the software decoder to improve compatibility, as hardware support is rare. (08a73f0)
    Media

Security & Privacy

  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in LegacyRenderWidgetHostHWND on Windows was fixed by adding WeakPtr guards to handle object destruction during synchronous Win32 calls. (73cfb55)
    SecurityWindowsUI
  • A security vulnerability was fixed where a compromised renderer could bypass microphone permission checks on Android. The browser now explicitly rejects speech recognition requests that attempt this bypass. (a10d218)
    SecurityAndroidMedia
  • A fix was implemented on Android to prevent a malicious webpage from using the soft keyboard to obscure the fullscreen entry notification, which could enable UI spoofing. (fb7aa25)
    SecurityAndroidUI
  • On iOS, regular expressions in WKContentRuleList are now anchored to prevent malicious URLs from bypassing blocking rules via query strings or fragments. (c8fe8b3)
    SecurityiOS
  • Several vulnerabilities were addressed in Chromecast, including potential navigation provenance laundering, a heap buffer overflow, an out-of-bounds read, and a potential use-after-free in the BLE implementation. (cd5d219) (6183ca6) (978af60) (ecf7dbf)
    SecurityMediaBluetooth

Embedder APIs, UI & Services

  • A new, safer CreateBubble method that returns a std::unique_ptr<Widget> was introduced to modernize bubble creation and help prevent memory leaks. (1a3139e)
    UIBase
  • On iOS, the web::WebFrame API was enhanced with new CallAsyncJavaScriptFunction methods to allow native code to call JavaScript functions that return Promises. (dd55163)
    iOS
  • For Android WebView, support was added for the new AndroidX navigate API, providing a more structured way for embedders to trigger navigations. (4a35add)
    Android
  • New context menu items "Copy video frame" and "Download video frame" have been implemented for videos on Android. (7062176) (839877c)
    AndroidUIMedia
  • A new framework for automatically recording animation performance metrics has been introduced, and several UI components like the side panel and vertical tab strip have been migrated to use it. (c83c394) (e8eb851) (7596b60)
    UIPerformance

Build & Infrastructure

  • The Mold linker is now enabled for non-official arm64 Android builds to improve link times. (846f170) (563140c)
    BuildAndroid
  • A large number of expired or launched feature flags were removed from the codebase, including web-serial-wired-devices-android, FledgeTextConversionHelpers, FedCmEmbedderCheck, and reintroduce-hybrid-passkey-entry-point. (1113e33) (c843cd5) (c2302d0) (faeef77)
    Build
  • As part of a larger modularization effort, circular dependencies were broken between //chrome/browser and several sub-components, including spellchecker, gpu, and segmentation_platform. (047a726) (8b96916) (755f4c3)
    BuildInfra
  • The DevTools logic for Page.addScriptToEvaluateOnNewDocument was refactored to be maintained on the browser side instead of the renderer, improving robustness. (cf0d873)
    DevTools

2026-05-05

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-05

Overview

A summary of 803 commits on the main branch, with 614 considered relevant. This digest focuses on developer-impacting changes, including an unusually high number of breaking API modifications across multiple components. The first relevant commit was (3f267f3) and the last was (38c1ba6).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public C++ interface cc::LayerTreeHostSchedulingClient has been renamed to cc::LayerTreeHostSchedulingDelegate. Embedders implementing this compositor scheduling interface must update their class definitions. (53e1246)
    CompositorBlink
  • ⚠️The signatures of several public API functions in the extensions subsystem, such as AllowCrossRendererResourceLoad, have been changed to use the strongly-typed content::ChildProcessId instead of a raw int. (d348d44)
    Extensions
  • ⚠️The extensions::mojom::EventRouter interface was refactored, moving service_worker_context from a struct to a direct parameter on IPC methods. Additionally, the C++ API extensions::MimeTypesHandler::GetHandler() now returns a const pointer. (907c3bf) (a086295)
    ExtensionsServiceWorker
  • ⚠️The virtual method SharedImageBackingFactory::CreateSharedImage now accepts a gpu::SharedImageInfo struct instead of individual parameters. Custom factory implementations must be updated. (c06e765)
    GPUCompositor
  • ⚠️The method signature for DirectRenderer::DrawFrame, a core public class in the Viz display component, has been changed to add a new const AggregatedFrame& parameter. Subclasses must be updated. (e14bcb1)
    CompositorGPU
  • ⚠️On macOS, synchronous methods in the public TextInputClientMac interface have been renamed (e.g., GetCharacterIndexAtPoint is now SyncGetCharacterIndexAtPoint) and new asynchronous versions have been introduced. Embedders must update their calls. (60aa06c)
    macOSUI
  • ⚠️Several SuggestionGenerator methods in Autofill now accept a non-const AutofillClient*. Separately, the public AutocompleteInput class was refactored to replace an enum with a boolean for keyword mode. (4bd9046) (1a40eab)
    AutofillUI
