  • ⚠️The virtual method permissions::PermissionContextBase::NotifyPermissionSet has a new const content::PermissionResult* parameter. Subclasses must update their overrides. (5f2014a)
    BlinkPrivacy
  • ⚠️Focus-related methods (FocusInactivePopupForAccessibility, RotatePaneFocus, etc.) have been removed from the public BrowserWindow interface and moved to a new BrowserFocusController class. (16c69f3)
    UIBrowserAccessibility
  • ⚠️The public low-level type alias subtle::AtomicWord has been removed from base/atomicops.h, which will break any code that uses it directly. (a9b7f6a)
    Base
  • ⚠️On Android, the OMA DRM download feature has been completely removed. Separately, the behavior of Navigate() has been altered, as the browser parameter is now optional, which may cause some navigations to open a new window unexpectedly. (a491df8) (0b478c5)
    AndroidDownloadsNavigation
  • ⚠️On ChromeOS, the ability to install Debian (.deb) packages directly from the Files app UI has been removed and is now blocked with a warning dialog. (fb90834)
    ChromeOSLinux
  • ⚠️The constructor for web::ScriptMessage in the public iOS embedder API now requires a url::Origin security_origin parameter. (57033eb)
    iOS
  • ⚠️The underlying map types for icon storage in the public web_app_icon_types.h and dialog_image_info.h headers have been changed, breaking code that relies on the specific std::map implementation. (e6b16cf) (e75b189)
    UI

Security and Privacy

  • The Web Share API is now hardened in the browser process, which will validate provided URLs and terminate a compromised renderer attempting to use non-web schemes like file://. (3f267f3)
    SecurityBlink
  • A potential NTLM credential leak has been mitigated in the getFileSystemInfo enterprise API by adding path validation to block network paths. (e04526f)
    EnterpriseSecurityNetwork
  • A fix in the Windows MachineIdProvider prevents a heap out-of-bounds read when a storage driver returns a malformed descriptor header. (236ef5f)
    SecurityWindows
  • Web Bluetooth will now correctly abort pending watchAdvertisements requests when a page loses visibility, preventing a potential privacy bypass. (e6b4397)
    BluetoothPrivacy
  • The maximum size of HID report descriptors is now limited to 65535 bytes to prevent out-of-memory or timeout failures during parsing. (e2d3268)
    SecurityUSB

Web Platform

  • The CSSSafePrintableInset feature is now enabled by default, shipping support for the @page descriptor page-margin-safety for controlling print margins. (798c128)
    BlinkCSSPrinting
  • The image.decode() API will now correctly resolve its promise even if the image decode fails due to memory constraints, aligning with the specification. (cb0c0d8)
    BlinkHTML
  • A bug in AudioBufferSourceNode has been fixed where the duration was incorrectly handled when looping with a playbackRate other than 1.0, ensuring playback stops correctly. (381fd85)
    BlinkMedia
  • Icon URL schemes in Web App Manifests are now restricted to http, https, data, or the document's scheme to mitigate Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) risks. (7cb6a9b)
    BlinkSecurity
  • Support for MIPS intrinsic optimizations in WebAudio has been removed. Builds on MIPS will now fall back to a scalar implementation, which may cause a performance regression for WebAudio on that architecture. (3b8474a)
    MediaPerformance

Platforms

  • On Android, the native PDF viewer is now loaded on demand to reduce initial binary size. Additionally, Seamless Signin is now enabled by default. (15ba7f5) (911787f)
    AndroidPDF
  • On Android, the TextBubble component now uses a Builder pattern, and its previous public constructors have been deprecated. (efa0aa1)
    AndroidUI
  • On iOS, the GuidedTourCoordinator was refactored to use a block-based completion handler instead of a delegate protocol, simplifying its usage. (180543e)
    iOSUI
  • On Windows, a bug was fixed where an audio device could incorrectly inherit the friendly name of a previously enumerated device. (cdc79f9)
    WindowsMedia

UI and Features

  • The BrowserAnimationController has been significantly enhanced to support persisting animation values and blending between interrupted and new animations, simplifying future animation development. (388ebfb)
    UIBrowser
  • The success toast message for the "Send Tab to Self" feature will now include the name of the target device, providing better user feedback. (29e7f72)
    UISync
  • The idle timeout for voice search has been increased from 1.5s to 3s, and the "Stop" button has been visually updated to match the "Submit" button. (abeda71) (1dfd94c)
    UI
  • Several feature flags have been removed as their features are now stable and enabled by default, including TopControlsRefactor (Android), openxr-spatial-entities, InfobarRefresh, and ReduceUserAgentDataLinuxPlatformVersion. (b7884eb) (aa16f85) (69e4d33) (9b5171f)
    UIAndroidXR

Build and Internals

  • A new check in the SQLite VFS disallows multiple unsynchronized connections to the same sandboxed database, which would previously lead to likely database corruption. (92e25b0)
    StorageBuild
  • The proto_library GN template was updated to propagate dependencies publicly, ensuring that transitive headers are correctly resolved during compilation and fixing certain build failures. (05dab77)
    BuildInfra
  • A new ContentBrowserClient hook, GetEffectiveTopFrameForPartitioning, was added to allow embedders to define the effective top-level frame for storage partitioning, primarily for use with MIME handler extensions. (eedec13) (45b03bb)
    ExtensionsStorage

2026-05-04

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-04

Overview

This daily summary covers 567 commits on the main branch from May 4, 2026, with 427 commits identified as relevant to developers. The changes span from commit (162a1ba) to (4802e28). A significant number of breaking changes were landed, impacting core compositor, UI, media, and autofill APIs.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️The public C++ function Atomic64::Acquire_Load has been removed from the core header base/atomicops.h. Embedders directly using this low-level atomic operation must migrate to a different implementation. (ef2ad96)
    Base
  • ⚠️The core compositor interface cc::LayerTreeHostImplClient has been renamed to cc::LayerTreeHostImplDelegate. Embedders implementing or referring to this class must update their code. (67cf5cf)
    CompositorBlink
  • ⚠️The public method SetVSyncDisplayID on several viz and gl interfaces (e.g., ui::gl::Presenter, viz::OutputSurface) now requires a force_update parameter, breaking existing calls and implementations. (a5ab2ae)
    GPUUImacOS
  • ⚠️The CanvasResourceProviderSharedImage interface has been refactored. The method GetSharedImageUsageFlags() is now a pure virtual, which will break custom canvas resource provider implementations. (f6ecb37) (2e24745)
    BlinkGPU
  • ⚠️The PasswordStoreConsumer interface has been updated to use StoredCredential. The public method OnGetPasswordStoreResultsOrErrorFrom now passes a vector of StoredCredential instead of PasswordForm, breaking custom consumers. (2b93d3b)
    PasswordManager
  • ⚠️The public method BrowserAutofillManager::DidShowSuggestions has changed its signature to accept a const FormGlobalId& instead of a const FormData&, requiring updates to any code calling it. (50dc42f)
    Autofill
  • ⚠️The public constructor for media::FFmpegVideoDecoder now takes a std::unique_ptr<MediaLog> instead of a raw MediaLog*. Embedders creating instances of this class directly must update their code. (16b2782)
    Media
  • ⚠️The static method views::BubbleDialogDelegate::CreateBubble has been renamed to CreateBubbleDeprecated. This is an API removal that will cause build failures for any code still calling the old method. (5ee0e6a)
    UI
  • ⚠️The StyleImage::IsCorsSameOrigin method signature has been changed, removing its String& parameter. Embedders subclassing or directly calling this Blink API will need to update their code. (644d893)
    BlinkCSS
  • ⚠️The public methods SetShouldShowLeadingSeparator and SetShouldShowTrailingSeparator have been removed from the MultiContentsView class. (04ff39d)
    UI

Blink & Web Platform

  • The CSS shape-outside property is now more powerful, with support for path(), shape(), rect(), and xywh() basic shapes enabled by default. (9611ae0) (ed8a8cb)
    BlinkCSS
  • A new JavaScript API, Element.matchContainer(), has been introduced behind the ElementMatchContainer flag, allowing developers to programmatically evaluate if an element satisfies the conditions of a CSS container query. (8308463)
    BlinkCSS
  • To align with web standards, <option> and <optgroup> elements inside a disabled <select> will now correctly match the :disabled CSS pseudo-class. (a76ffb0)
    BlinkCSS
  • The experimental accuracyMode attribute has been removed from the GeolocationPosition web API as part of a specification update. (e71de3b)
    BlinkPrivacy
  • The Gamepad API is gaining support for raw input events via a new Origin Trial, providing access to unmapped and unfiltered controller data. (4eac740)
    BlinkWebRTC
  • In WebAudio, memory allocation for PeriodicWave is now fallible. Instead of crashing on out-of-memory errors, it will now throw a NotSupportedError. (cd69090)
    BlinkMedia
  • The HTMLOrForeignElement IDL type has been renamed to HTMLOrSVGOrMathMLElement to align with WHATWG specifications. (0c96db4)
    BlinkHTML

Security

  • The browser process now strictly verifies the sessionId in all DevTools protocol messages from renderer-side sessions. A compromised renderer attempting to spoof or omit the sessionId will now be terminated. (de2829e)
    SecurityDevTools
  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in the passthrough command decoder's DoBufferData implementation has been patched. (1353277)
    SecurityGPU
  • A Use-After-Free in FileSelectHelper was fixed by replacing manual refcounting with a WebContents-scoped manager. (2e5d150)
    SecurityStorage
  • On iOS, the on-disk ArchivableCredentialStore file, which could contain plaintext passwords, is now explicitly excluded from device backups. (977f350)
    SecurityiOSPasswordManager
  • Renderer-initiated navigations from inactive RenderFrameHosts are now consistently disallowed to enhance security. (96f0257)
    SecurityNavigation

Core, Performance & GPU

  • On macOS, the CALayerTreeCoordinator now allocates a new CAContext on resize to ensure atomic updates between the browser and GPU processes, fixing potential flickering. (acca2e2)
    CompositorGPUmacOS
  • The CastStreamingMediaVideoEncoder feature is now enabled by default, improving performance for Cast streaming. (ee0ca84)
    MediaGPU
  • Several instances of SplitString were replaced with the more performant SplitStringPiece across the codebase, reducing string copies. (ca753e1) (2b78ba8) (baa0337)
    PerformanceBase
  • A data race in TaskAttributionId was fixed by making its internal ID generator atomic, improving scheduler reliability. (ca652e2)
    BasePerformance

UI & UX

  • On Android, new accessibility code paths using WindowContentChange events are now enabled by default, improving support for atomic live regions. (3514335)
    AccessibilityAndroid
  • The kTextSelectionChanged event is now generated for Views textfields, improving accessibility eventing for screen readers and other assistive tools. (de1e925)
    AccessibilityUI
  • On macOS, a new platform-specific view has been implemented for the PWA install dialog, and its progress bar is now synchronized with the actual installation process. (c9dcfdf) (e3a6456)
    UImacOS
  • For FedCM, pop-up windows will now open as a new tab when the user is in fullscreen mode to prevent UI lock-in. (e810103)
    UIPrivacy

Services

  • The PaymentRequestSupportReportingAppError feature is now enabled by default. This changes how payment handler installation failures are reported, now using OperationError instead of AbortError in some cases, which may affect web developers' error handling logic. (e7bf50b)
    PaymentsBlink
  • Support has been added to Safety Hub for autorevoking approximate/precise geolocation permissions, allowing users to manage granular location access more easily. (2b90bb7)
    PrivacyUI
  • SQL helper utilities from the Autofill component have been promoted to the common sql/ directory for broader use within Chromium. (a6cbd90) (548b964)
    AutofillStorageBase

Platforms

  • On Android, a regression where the text-size-adjust CSS property stopped working has been fixed. (da77078)
    AndroidCSS
  • On iOS, a cross-origin cache leakage vulnerability in the Virtual Card Number (VCN) manual fill feature was fixed. (978bf23)
    iOSSecurityPayments
  • On macOS, application audio capture has been updated to use a CaptureIdentifier that includes the bundle ID, allowing for more precise filtering of audio sources. (d07ac38) (8ca5da9)
    macOSMedia
  • On ChromeOS, the correct color space is now used for camera capture on Screebo devices to fix issues with YUV frames. (9773892)
    ChromeOSMedia

Build & Infrastructure

  • The extensions_browsertests binary is no longer built. Its tests have been successfully migrated into the main browser_tests suite. (3df42d8)
    BuildExtensionsTesting
  • The -gdwarf-aranges compiler flag is now disabled when use_dwarf5=true, significantly reducing binary size on x64 builds. (0f63b34)
    BuildInfra
  • The build system now includes routing to support the migration from TFLite to the new LiteRT library for on-device machine learning. (cc2787b)
    BuildAI
  • A large number of commits were dedicated to reverting changes that caused test failures and disabling flaky tests to stabilize the tree. Reverted features include a Mojo bridge for the system proxy resolver on macOS and a new native dialog for Glic. (f12b956) (928a673) (265034e) (81971ea)
    TestingInfra

2026-05-03

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2024-05-24

Overview

This digest covers 22 commits, highlighting a significant breaking change in the Autofill component. Other changes include performance improvements in Blink, internal refactoring in UI and preloading systems, and numerous updates to testing infrastructure.

Autofill

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Breaking Change: The DidShowSuggestions() method on BrowserAutofillManager is no longer virtual. Embedders who subclass BrowserAutofillManager and override this method will experience build failures. The recommended approach is to now use the AutofillManager::Observer pattern and listen for the OnSuggestionsShown event. (bc91d7f)

Blink & Web Platform

  • The LinkBlurImprovement and CommandEventNotComposed feature flags have been removed now that the features are stable and enabled by default. (d875b1e) (2e3ea7d)
  • A performance regression in <select> elements has been fixed. A microtask was being queued unnecessarily when a selected <option> was removed, which is now conditional, improving performance on related benchmarks. (b467ed4)

UI & Shell

  • Internal refactoring has moved the UnloadHandler to BrowserWindowFeatures as part of a larger effort to clean up Browser methods. No behavioral change is intended. (beaae0f)
  • A bug has been fixed where the Windows touch keyboard would ignore autocorrect="off" on input fields. The fix now correctly reverts autocorrect actions on such fields without affecting IME composition. (02d88f7)
  • Minor UI updates include a fix for the bottom margin on the Android hub search for tablets and a comment clarification for views::ScopedPaintLock. (8cb9687) (5e2ce33)

Preloading

  • The Off-Main-Thread Prefetch (OMTPrefetch) service has been improved to asynchronously refresh its header cache when headers are missing and to deduplicate in-flight requests for the same headers, improving efficiency. (c83f29a) (13008e3)

Internals & Infrastructure

  • The sandboxed SQLite VFS now treats zero-length files as non-existent. This aligns its behavior with standard SQLite VFS implementations and fixes a bug related to WAL mode detection. (c14174a)
  • The experimental WebNN backend for ONNX Runtime (ORT) has been refactored to allow initialization without a hard dependency on the WinML package, enabling its use on a wider range of Windows versions. (1607476)

Testing

  • A large number of Ash (Aura Shell) tests have been modernized by replacing broad RunUntilIdle calls with more targeted and reliable synchronization mechanisms like TestFuture and observer waiters. (c48a9d7) (565edba) (80b8cdc) (fdee5a6) (0d84854) (9a24f9d)
  • Web tests for the experimental html-in-canvas feature have been updated to use a more robust waitForCanvasPaint pattern instead of relying on requestAnimationFrame and setTimeout. (a14d323)

iOS

  • New unit tests have been added for the Page Action Menu's content entry point. A separate change enables translation for a snackbar related to Gemini account eligibility. (d7aa3d1) (dd25902)

Localization

  • Translation files (XTBs) have been updated from their corresponding string resource files (GRDs). (8f666cc)

2026-05-02

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2024-07-26

Overview

This digest covers 50 commits from the Chromium repository, ranging from (c45c88b) to (94371de). The analysis identifies no major breaking changes to stable public APIs. Key themes include security hardening against compromised renderers, significant progress on the experimental WebNN API, removal of the legacy Widevine test server, and numerous UI/UX refinements across desktop and mobile platforms.

Security

  • Hardening measures were implemented to better isolate processes. One change prevents a compromised renderer from manipulating subframe error page origins by forcing them to use a unique opaque origin without a precursor (470a5614ecfbdd85e5b0bb97719ffafa85410872). Another adds validation to the Extensions EventRouter to reject unauthorized IPC messages attempting to modify service worker event listeners, preventing state mutation by a compromised process (31dce68b925c2b8efc93df832a86a7c0d03e3fa2). (470a561) (31dce68)
  • Memory safety was improved in several components. A potential out-of-bounds access in the WebNN TFLite backend was fixed by adding an integer overflow check for TransposeConv2d operations (cb10fdc4932d59d0f208cb4de02c7447862f98cc). Separately, an uninitialized memory access in the macOS Gamepad API vibration implementation was resolved by ensuring the correct effect reference is checked before use (b90a779912796e317ee82c393a4c5cfcd3402347). (cb10fdc) (b90a779)

Web Platform & APIs

  • The experimental WebNN API continues to evolve with internal refactoring to create a GraphBuilderContext abstract interface, decoupling the graph builder from the context implementation (a588ac0281accdaf4291b021d483c773195d7cd4). Validation was also added to prevent out-of-bounds memory access in slice operations by ensuring strides do not exceed input tensor dimensions (3fafb5a4c8f1284a51e03cca5c18b4eb566b0469). (a588ac0) (3fafb5a)
  • Accessibility was improved for form controls. The calendar picker's month selector now uses aria-description instead of aria-label, allowing screen readers to announce the visible month (e2522c5c32a579bf477fddac9eaf9d4070cdfc20). Additionally, an experimental feature for platform-provided behaviors now automatically assigns default ARIA roles and keyboard focus behavior to custom elements, improving their accessibility without manual attribute management (63c8bd73b94fc810d9f467ca05d6c71cea48038a). (e2522c5) (63c8bd7)
  • The behavior of the <select> listbox has been updated to improve keyboard navigation consistency. Focus now moves to the same option in the listbox whether tabbing forward or backward, and the selected option receives initial focus, matching the behavior of dropdown selects (36b6cd7fa754d907e1aac01157e67c23f16713dd). (36b6cd7)
  • The Additional Windowing Controls API now rejects promises on the renderer side if a web app is not in the correct display mode (e.g., calling window.maximize() in browser mode). This provides faster and more consistent feedback to developers (b292bdd608d8ae57ce16b8b818dd23654009f8bd). (b292bdd)
  • Support for ARCore-backed OpenXR on Android is being enhanced by adding new library dependencies to the XR module, specifically androidx.xr.arcore.openxr and androidx_xr_runtime_runtime_openxr_java. (4a37d42) (a63b0e5)

UI & UX

  • The New Tab Page (NTP) is being refined by enabling the removal of shortcuts by default in preparation for launch (3a3611726af01d8d2e15d259cd427f99e89427d5) and disabling the "AI mode" button's animation on initial page load for a cleaner experience (759832c57904cf108089236d59e4af1c9eb296e3). (3a36117) (759832c)
  • On Android, the toast notification for a "new tab opened in background" has been repositioned to always appear at least 16dp above the highest bottom controls, preventing it from being obscured (2ea34692ca4005ac6985947a555c87e2d94f32ac). The IPH text for the back gesture has also been updated for better accessibility and localization (8f3de43050e95b0353fb20e2d87dd8c57111afb7). (2ea3469) (8f3de43)
  • The Omnibox popup is being refactored to replace the existing cr-searchbox with a new, lightweight omnibox-popup-searchbox component, which is part of a broader modernization effort (a8bf85ad7e9fe709ffa098e4a6606457f2dcd731). (a8bf85a)
  • The Windows updater UI now supports high-contrast mode by adopting dynamic system colors and redrawing controls when the OS theme changes (3517cbea33d13b36f48bcd60a5579a80502e58ef). (3517cbe)

Media

  • The Widevine test license server infrastructure, which was Linux-only and difficult to maintain, has been removed. This includes its entry in DEPS and all related implementation files. Encrypted media tests that relied on this server have been updated to expect failure or be skipped, aligning behavior across platforms and simplifying the test infrastructure. This change affects internal testing but is not a breaking change for production embedders. (1262aed)
  • A bug was fixed on Android where MediaFeatureList flags were not receiving Finch configurations on the Java side because they were not registered in ChromeCachedFlags. This ensures media-related feature flags can be controlled remotely as intended. (bbc8f8f)

Extensions

  • A crash in the EventRouter has been fixed. The crash occurred when an extension tried to remove a stale, in-memory event listener whose filter was no longer present in persisted preferences. The code now gracefully handles cases where the filter is not found, preventing the crash. (ce6c6b2)

Internals & Refactoring

  • A bug causing full-page blanking during screenshot redaction has been fixed. The redaction logic now correctly copies the original bitmap before drawing redaction masks, ensuring the rest of the page content remains visible. (c1c7fa4)
  • Significant code cleanup was performed, including the removal of dead code and unused setters from the Android Omnibox StatusView (a09d0bb8652b884d5e309aea1b662218a21632ca, caf1fd12ebdc974f9d5fdba7c57eaf114ffe2fde) and the removal of a now-unnecessary dangling pointer check in tips_client following an underlying fix (58d983610dd06d3510c202cc7d5764526e97a402). (a09d0bb) (caf1fd1) (58d9836)

Testing & Infrastructure

  • PWA browser tests have been made more robust by replacing flaky ad-hoc waits with a new WebAppPageWaiter utility. This utility uses a new event on WebAppTabHelper to reliably wait for all web app system activity to be scheduled for a given page. (4b3f90b)
  • A new unit test suite has been introduced for ContentWebState on iOS. This allows for better testing of content::WebContents behavior in an iOS Blink unit test environment, starting with tests for visibility methods. (19f244e)

2026-05-01

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-05-01

Overview

A busy day with 603 commits, of which 431 were deemed relevant for this summary. The day saw a significant number of breaking API changes and refactorings, alongside security enhancements and new features. Activity spanned from the first commit 2b2726ca9d5289a827b6802d96192467d2aa6caa to the last 22921c2492c410545d874d82e16946f48c678ad5.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core compositor class cc::LayerTreeHostClient has been renamed to cc::LayerTreeHostDelegate. Embedders implementing this class for LayerTreeHost must update to the new name. (afd22cd)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The enterprise policy ForceForegroundPriorityForOrigins is renamed to ForceForegroundPriorityForUrls. Its matching logic now uses full URLs instead of origins, requiring administrators to update policy configurations. (605fbfc)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING For non-branded builds, the default Google crash server URL has been removed. Embedders must now explicitly configure their own crash reporting endpoint. (e7f4186)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API WebView.findAddress has been removed from Android WebView. This was obsolete on Android P+ but is a breaking change for embedders targeting older versions. (35557c4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature of the public virtual method PdfInkModuleClient::DrawText has been changed to include a new InkTextId parameter. Embedders implementing this interface must update their code. (4ae99a5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for views::BubbleDialogDelegate::CreateBubble has changed. The default parameter for widget ownership was removed, and all callers must now explicitly provide it. (3301350)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Method signatures in the public speech recognition API, including SpeechRecognitionManagerDelegate, have been changed to use content::GlobalRenderFrameHostId instead of separate render_process_id and render_frame_id integers. (5ee42da) (c15d856)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method BrowserView::toolbar_height_side_panel() has been renamed to BrowserView::side_panel(). Code using this accessor for the browser's side panel must be updated. (49935f8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public class remoting::RemoteAudioInput has been renamed to remoting::AudioInjector. Embedders using the remoting component's audio injection feature must update to the new class name. (6b2679d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The JNI generation contract has been changed. The module_name must now be specified in the generate_jni GN build target instead of the @NativeMethods Java annotation, requiring updates to both source and build files. (629046f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public constructor HashValue(base::span<const uint8_t> hash) has been removed from net/base/hash_value.h. Code must be updated to use the HashValue(HashValueTag, base::span) constructor instead. (a91b8f4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public methods set_should_show_outline and should_show_outline have been removed from the SidePanelEntry UI class. (b105956)

Security

  • A check for invalid navigation headers sent by a renderer process was upgraded from a DumpWithoutCrashing to a full renderer process kill, hardening the browser against misbehaving renderers. (e0a0f3e)
  • A use-after-free vulnerability in the macOS implementation of FilePathWatcherFSEvents was fixed by introducing a ref-counted context. (cb6f7a4)
  • A potential local privilege escalation vulnerability in Chrome Remote Desktop on Windows was fixed by preventing the service from following reparse points when writing configuration files. (f67fb61)
  • A sanitization bypass in FrameSerializer was fixed. Mixed-case scripting attributes (e.g., ONLOAD) in MHTML files could previously lead to script execution on re-opening. (19cb047)
  • Security for the Service Worker Static Routing API was strengthened to prevent a Cross-Origin Resource Policy (CORP) bypass. (5046d3c)
  • Validation for main thread event listener IPCs in EventRouter was strengthened to prevent a compromised renderer from spoofing extension events. (6271562)
  • Support for ML-DSA signatures in TLS has been added behind a feature flag. (a2638b6)

Web Platform & Blink

  • The CSSGapDecoration feature for styling gaps in flexbox and grid layouts has been marked as stable and is now enabled by default. (4934ed0)
  • The window.fence.notifyEvent API and related Fenced Frames code have been removed, as the feature is no longer launching. (3cca452)
  • A new tools permissions policy was introduced for the Web Model Context API (WebMCP), allowing cross-origin iframes to register and use tools when granted permission. (ea1d52c)
  • The chrome.histograms WebUI API has been updated to accept floating-point values, which are rounded to the nearest integer. This improves usability with APIs like performance.now(). (1622742)
  • Support for the spaces value in the ruby-overhang CSS property was added behind a feature flag. (85c5a07)

Android & WebView

  • The Web Feed feature has been removed, including its network/storage code and the kWebFeedContentOrder preference, as part of its ongoing deprecation. (c6e0135)
  • In WebView, Google Mobile Services (GMS) calls are now deferred until after Chromium startup by default to improve performance. This is controlled by the WebViewDeferStartupGmsCalls flag. (305be3b)
  • The Android contact picker will use the native system picker on Android 17+ when a feature flag is enabled, removing the need for the READ_CONTACTS permission. (30c0d18)
  • A prototype of a WebUI-based New Tab Page is being introduced, guarded by a GN argument and a runtime flag. (42937e4)
  • A crash in the Android BottomSheet was fixed, which occurred when changing device orientation while the keyboard was visible. (f2f79ca)

UI & UX

  • The voice search UI has been updated with a new "Aurora" recording animation and an explicit stop/submit button, gated by a feature flag. (efb148a) (6a4b098)
  • The Side Panel Bookmarks "edit dialog" was migrated from Polymer to Lit. (6786396)
  • A fix was implemented for the placement of window menus on Linux with X11, which were appearing in the wrong location due to a DIP vs. pixel coordinate mix-up. (3880f83)
  • Text annotations in the PDF viewer can now be deleted with the Backspace key in addition to the Delete key. (3790f18)
  • A race condition was fixed in tab dragging where detaching tabs could fail if the tab strip's selection model changed during the drag operation. (9000f56)

Extensions

  • A change that required a user gesture for the addHostAccessRequest API was reverted, restoring the ability for extensions to call it programmatically. (a76e95f)
  • The nacl_arch property in the runtime.getPlatformInfo() API has been deprecated, as Native Client (NaCl) was removed in M139. (6f14e2a)

Code Health & Refactoring

  • The browser_navigator files have been moved from chrome/browser/ui/ to a new chrome/browser/ui/navigator/ subdirectory, requiring updates to include paths. (815c5cf)
  • Code related to glic and lens has been modularized out of the main //chrome/browser target into their own source sets to improve dependency management. (4646bd8) (bc61a54)
  • A large number of tests in ash/ were migrated away from using the generic RunUntilIdle() to more targeted and reliable synchronization primitives, improving test stability. (78dd3d4) (60e3a08) (53b6e88)
  • A new SQL-based shared HTTP cache backend was implemented to map NetworkIsolationKey to a database ID, part of ongoing networking stack improvements. (7a02630)

2026-04-30

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-30

Overview

This summary covers 745 commits on the main branch, with 556 identified as relevant to developers. The changes span from April 29, 2026 ((084453c)) to April 30, 2026 ((5186844)). The day was marked by an unusually high number of breaking changes across the stack, impacting public C++, Mojo, and component APIs.

Major Breaking Changes & API Removals

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The WebUI Tab Strip feature has been completely removed. Builds enabling this feature via the enable_webui_tab_strip GN argument will fail. All associated files, including tab_strip.mojom and tab_strip_api.mojom, have been deleted. (17de746)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public AIProofreader mojom API has been changed to support batching. GetCorrectionType was removed and replaced by GetCorrectionsTypes, which has a different signature. (b8be3ba)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The core webapps::ManifestId type was refactored from a GURL alias to a webapps::ValidManifestId wrapper class. This breaks all call sites that assumed it was a GURL, requiring code changes to handle the new type and its validation logic. (73319ea)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public, exported function base::DateIntervalFormat was removed from the base library. Any code relying on this function will fail to compile. (1c49b4d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The webstorePrivate extension API permission has been removed. This is a public API removal that could break external extensions that were on the allowlist. (de56b30)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The non-WebRTC transport protocol support in Remoting has been removed. The public SessionConfig and CandidateSessionConfig classes and associated methods have been deleted from remoting protocol interfaces, breaking custom transport integrations. (32d22ac)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public utility function startup::ValidateUrl has been renamed to startup::ValidateLaunchUrl. Callers must be updated to use the new name. (133f8ae)

Blink, Rendering & GPU

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public C++ API for VulkanImage::Create and VulkanImage::CreateWithExternalMemory has been changed. The extra_image_create_info and extra_memory_allocation_info parameters were removed. (27868bf)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public API of SharedImageStub has been reduced. The method GetSharedImageDestructionCallback and type alias SharedImageDestructionCallback were removed, and CreateSharedImage and UpdateSharedImage are now private. (e7206ff)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: An overload of the public, exported function NextPositionOf has been removed from Blink's core editing header visible_units.h as part of a larger refactoring. (57595fe)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The response callback has been removed from the public Mojo method FrameWidgetInputHandler::PasteFromImageBytes, making image insertion a silent-fail operation. (6f76ae0)
  • The HarfBuzz text shaping library was rolled forward, incorporating a new hb-gpu module for GPU glyph encoding. (1ceca47)
  • The focusgroup feature was updated to exclude top-layer elements like popovers from ancestor navigation, and a bug was fixed for tabbing out of elements with tabindex=-1. (df80c56) (135c5a3)
  • For privacy, visited link colors are now ignored for elements within a canvas subtree. (8d8719a)
  • The Vertical Tabs feature is now enabled by default on Windows, Mac, and Linux. (20e810a)

Services & Components

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public method send_tab_to_self::SendTabToSelfModel::AddEntry was renamed to SendEntry. Embedders using this component must update their code. (2576012)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The pure virtual method GetPlusAddressHatsData was removed from the public plus_addresses::PlusAddressService interface as part of a feature removal. Implementations of this service will need to be updated. (2465284)
  • BREAKING: The signature of the public observer method PageContentExtractionService::Observer::OnPageContentExtracted has changed. Existing implementations will break and must be updated to handle the new PageContent variant. (2f7a652)
  • Several components, including the GPU ProgramCache and Omnibox OnDeviceTailModelService, were migrated from the deprecated base::MemoryPressureListener to the new base::MemoryConsumer API. (1c39e4f) (6c95781)
  • The core browser navigator logic was extracted into a dedicated //chrome/browser/ui/navigator build target to improve modularity. (a7ea861)

Networking & Security

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A new required method, OnPlatformLocalNetworkPermissionRequired, has been added to the public URLLoaderNetworkServiceObserver Mojo interface. This will cause compilation failures for existing implementers, who must now handle Local Network Access (LNA) permission requests. (3218389)
  • A significant security fix addresses a CSP bypass in Manifest V2 extensions where directives like script-src-elem were not properly sanitized, potentially allowing remote code execution. (99e2591)
  • To prevent information leaks, URL fragments, usernames, and passwords are now stripped from Cross-Origin Embedder Policy (COEP) and Document Isolation Policy (DIP) violation reports. (59bf0e2)
  • A data leak bypass was fixed for file selections from guest VMs (Crostini) by correctly setting the dialog caller context, ensuring Data Loss Prevention (DLP) checks are applied. (e52de61)
  • A Web Share API vulnerability was fixed, preventing a page from sharing file: URLs by manipulating the <base> tag. (65fe959)

Extensions & Media

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Support for CDM Host versions older than 10 has been removed, simplifying the Clear Key CDM implementation. This may break integrations relying on older CDM host versions. (dc13d18)
  • The MIME handler infrastructure for extensions was refactored to route generic handlers through the OOPIF (Out-of-Process iframe) path, improving security and isolation. (d680baa) (e8aa93f)
  • A bug was fixed where a renderer process could remove a webRequest listener belonging to another renderer; removals are now correctly scoped to the originating process. (be668fb)
  • The Widevine CDM for Linux arm64 is now bundled with the browser. (e94ddd2)

Platform-Specific

  • Android: The AutocompleteController API was updated to accept a WebContents parameter, enabling more contextual suggestions. (2b80341) (c96df14) (4924e16)
  • Android: Navigation logic was changed to prevent opening new tabs in non-normal windows (like PWAs or popups), falling back to a tabbed browser window instead. (d03f492)
  • Android: A memory leak in ActivityLifecycleDispatcherImpl was fixed by ensuring all ObserverList fields are cleared during dispatchOnDestroy. (f944c9f)
  • iOS: The Composebox (AIM) feature received numerous updates, including model/tool selection UI, state integration, and bug fixes. (fe189dc) (6eb4b1c)
  • iOS: The Tab Switcher Overflow Menu is now enabled by default. (0113879) (d4b0cb7)
  • ChromeOS: For enterprise users, the Local Password and PIN (LPP) feature flags were enabled. (8741038)

2026-04-29

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-29

Overview

A busy day with 699 total commits, of which 518 were considered relevant after filtering. The day was marked by a very high number of breaking changes to core APIs in areas like the compositor, UI, base library, and networking. Major refactors also continued in the layout engine and browser-finding logic. The first commit was (9bc9e6b) and the last was (7eab0f4).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING In Blink's layout engine, several public virtual methods on LayoutBoxModelObject (e.g., BorderTop(), PaddingRight()) have been removed. Code must be updated to use the consolidated BorderOutsets() and PaddingOutsets() methods. (b2246d7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Key public virtual methods on the compositor's LayerTreeHostImpl (e.g., BeginCommit, CreatePendingTree) have been moved to a new ClientLayerTreeHostImpl subclass. Embedders who subclass or directly call these methods must update to the new class. (144eb65)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for SharedImageFactory::CreateSharedImage has changed. It now accepts a SharedImageInfo struct instead of a long list of parameters. (08ee0a7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public methods related to fullscreen state (e.g., ShouldHideUIForFullscreen()) have been removed from the BrowserWindow and Browser interfaces. This functionality now resides in the new BrowserWindowFullscreenController. (d1db08e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The widely used function chrome::FindBrowserWithTab is being removed. Embedders must migrate to new APIs like GlobalBrowserCollection to find a Browser* from a WebContents*. (48bd889)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base::RandInt function has been removed from base/rand_util.h. Callers must migrate to base::RandIntInclusive. (5f591f6)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The password_manager::PasswordStoreConsumer interface was changed, replacing the virtual method OnGetPasswordStoreResults with OnGetPasswordStoreResultsOrErrorFrom. (18f3b6c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public method writable_hdr_metadata() has been removed from the media::VideoDecoderConfig class. (5794b9e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The MADV_FREE-based discardable memory feature, including the public function base::GetDiscardableMemoryBacking(), has been removed from base. (73c9221)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Legacy non-WebRTC remoting components have been removed, including public headers and the CreateChannelAuthenticator() pure virtual method from remoting::protocol::Authenticator. (e548872)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public RTCLoggingDispatcher Mojo interface was modified. FinishDiagnosticLogging now requires a metadata parameter, and the Mojo file's directory was renamed, breaking include paths. (8e0ea31)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Android, the SideUiObserver interface method onPreSideUiSpecsChange now returns an android.transition.Transition instead of an android.animation.Animator. (3f4d377)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public AndroidAutofillClient class now inherits from content::WebContentsObserver, altering its class hierarchy and requiring subclasses to handle new lifecycle methods. (7ae919c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The behavior of RTCPeerConnection has changed with the enforcement of the Connection-Allowlist header. Construction can now be blocked by this header, which may require server-side configuration changes. (ec55185)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The behavior of ProtocolHandlerRegistry for Off-The-Record (OTR) profiles has changed. The registry is no longer shared with the original profile, isolating handlers. (7495f64)

Security

  • The RejectWeakCiphertext feature, which blocks weak symmetric ciphers in TLS, is now enabled by default. (5b951ab)
  • A logic flaw in DevTools that could leak raw header access and HttpOnly cookies after a session was detached has been fixed. (b1cb06e)
  • The Omnibox SearchSuggestionParser now validates that navigation suggestions use only HTTP or HTTPS schemes, preventing execution of malicious javascript: URLs. (f1feef3)
  • A Use-After-Free vulnerability in AXTreeSourceAndroid was fixed by ensuring a raw pointer is not retained across tree mutations that could invalidate it. (55bfe49)
  • Input validation was added to the VAAPI video decoders and encoders for AV1, H264, and VP8 to reject malformed data and prevent potential crashes or vulnerabilities. (e616e27) (1647d70) (390e0ed)

Blink & Web Platform

  • Support was added for calc() expressions that mix <angle> and <percentage> types in conic-gradient() color stops, aligning with the CSS Images 4 specification. (2f0622a)
  • The CSS line-clamp feature had its continue and block-ellipsis keywords renamed to align with new specification resolutions. (10dbb90)
  • A new kWebNNModelCompilation sandbox type was added for the WebNN API. This will be used for an upcoming compiler utility process to isolate ONNX Runtime graph compilation. (1adb86c)
  • The streaming sanitizer was updated to better handle text node normalization, aligning its behavior more closely with the HTML parser. (5aa3eb2)

UI & UX

  • The API to play specific segments of a Lottie animation (playSegments) has been made public, allowing for more granular control over animations. (bc01b08)
  • A tab's memory usage is now displayed on the tab hover card UI. (605b071)
  • A new feature flag, kWebUIOmniboxFullPopupV2, was introduced to allow parallel development on a next-generation WebUI omnibox popup. (099d60d)
  • The TranslateLanguageListModel interface was updated to expose language codes via GetTargetLanguageCodeAt, preparing for a new searchable language selection UI. (210dc00)

Extensions

  • Support for adding custom context menu items to the browser's tab context menu has been implemented. Extensions with the contextMenus permission can specify "tab" as a context, enabled by the kExtensionTabContextMenu feature flag. (ad49992)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Method signatures in the public extensions::ProcessMap API were changed to use the strongly-typed content::ChildProcessId instead of int. (2686e85)
  • A use-after-free vulnerability in UserScriptSet::UpdateUserScripts was fixed by ensuring memory is handled correctly during script updates. (fe455e2)

Mobile (Android & iOS)

  • On Android, the UnthrottleAsyncTouchMoves feature is now enabled by default (excluding WebView) to improve gesture responsiveness. (d40e41a)
  • On Android, the location bar was refactored, centralizing phone-specific button logic into the LocationBarCoordinator. (2d80323) (cf69f5d) (829b178)
  • On iOS, support for native map previews via universal links has been added. (0440587)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On iOS, the public virtual method BuildExternalPrivacyContext was removed from the SystemIdentityManager class. (6246ffa)

Code Removals & Infrastructure

  • Support for the pre-mmap Safe Browsing file format has been removed. Instead of migrating, Chromium will now return an error, prompting a full database update. (158b83a)
  • The Single Web Feed feature, previously used for the Creator Page in the Feed, has been removed along with its associated code and metrics. (7532c85)
  • The build system's proto_library template was updated to publicly propagate dependencies, fixing build issues where transitive headers could not be found. (7107857)
  • The unused translation_unit tool has been removed from the codebase. (0f53c60)

2026-04-27

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-27

Overview

This report highlights a significant number of breaking API changes across Android sign-in, core C++ components, Web Crypto, and the removal of the Web Feed feature, all requiring developer action. Alongside these refactors, numerous security vulnerabilities were patched in core libraries and APIs, and progress continued on emerging web platform features like CSS Container Queries and the <install> element. Of the 571 total commits on the main branch, 427 were deemed relevant. The first commit was 398bf4389292a344f5bdbfcc196ee3f723af36bc and the last was 34d0ce4055b12f4b3049705d22eb7cfe5285250f.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public Java interfaces for sign-in on Android, IdentityManager and SigninManager, have been significantly refactored. The deprecated ConsentLevel enum and its associated methods have been removed, other unused methods like getManagementDomain() were deleted, and several Callback interfaces were simplified or made non-nullable. Embedders using these APIs will need to update their calls to getPrimaryAccountInfo, hasPrimaryAccount, and sign-out flows. (22ad081) (11da9dd) (1c80e19) (436359d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several core C++ components received breaking API changes. ContentBrowserClient has a new request_destination parameter in RegisterNonNetworkWorkerMainResourceURLLoaderFactories. The persistent_cache component's Bind and MakeBackend methods now return base::expected instead of std::unique_ptr. The cc component's ScrollEventMetrics::Create* methods now require a scroll_begin_arrival_timestamp parameter. (0e9234d) (c426fc2) (9dd4ef9)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A new pure virtual method, IsDocumentPictureInPictureBlockedBySystem, was added to the public content::WebContentsDelegate interface. Embedders who implement this delegate must provide an implementation to avoid build failures. (bcd4839)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The "Web Feed" (Following) feature has been removed entirely. This deletes the components/feed/core/v2/web_feed_subscriptions module, its public APIs, and the kWebFeedSort feature flag. Any code depending on these components will fail. (3c759c0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public Web Crypto API has been updated. The IDL for SubtleCrypto.generateKey now returns a more specific Promise<(CryptoKey or CryptoKeyPair)> instead of Promise<any>. The underlying C++ blink::WebCryptoResult interface was also modified, which will break embedders using it directly. (b192c58)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public C++ interfaces for password and credential management have been changed. The sync_completion callback was removed from PasswordStoreInterface::RemoveLoginsCreatedBetween. For Device Bound Session Credentials, methods on UnexportableKeyProvider were renamed (e.g., DeleteSigningKeysSlowly to DeleteKeysSlowly), and the crypto::UnexportableKey interface was refactored. (a0f57d1) (2763d6c) (e94b66b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public APIs for Android features were removed or changed. The textFragmentLookup APIs were removed from CustomTabsConnection, and the AndroidIntentLauncher and DialogController Java interfaces for payments now use a PaymentAppError object instead of a String in error callbacks. (4d564e1) (75e28c3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Obsolete fields related to non-Fluent Windows scrollbar metrics (e.g., vertical_scroll_bar_width_in_dips) have been removed from the public blink::RendererPreferences struct. Embedders populating or reading these fields will encounter build errors. (07c1a24)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The chrome.virtualKeyboard and chrome.virtualKeyboardPrivate APIs have been removed from desktop platforms (Windows, Mac, Linux) as Chrome Apps are deprecated on these platforms. (ac2eccb)

Security

  • Patches were applied to third-party libraries to fix an integer truncation vulnerability in libxml2 and a type confusion issue in libxslt. (240a663) (f022d55)
  • Origin and policy enforcement was strengthened. A Cross-Origin Resource Policy (CORP) bypass in the Service Worker Static Router was fixed, stricter same-origin checks were added for SharedWorkers in Isolated Web Apps, and a check was added to the File System Access API to prevent a cross-origin token bypass when moving files. (b671011) (0e9234d) (3b8f2da)
  • Several components were hardened against renderer compromises. InputRouterImpl was protected against a Use-After-Free, Mojo services in the media stack now use mojo::ReportBadMessage() to avoid browser crashes, and WebGL was hardened to prevent enabling non-WebGL extensions via glRequestExtensionCHROMIUM. (33e1063) (3fc8eee) (7bdc81e)
  • A data race and potential heap-use-after-free in base::FormatDouble was fixed by replacing a global ICU NumberFormat instance with a thread-local pointer. (4ee829d)

Blink & Web Platform

  • The experimental ::interest-hint pseudo-element has been renamed to ::interest-button to align with the latest W3C proposal. (e9430ad) (bb7289f)
  • Support was added for CSSContainerRule.conditions to align with the upcoming comma-separated @container queries feature. (7d1eded)
  • The experimental <install> element now correctly exposes a "button" accessibility role. (8cd5386)
  • The Selector() function on CSSContainerRule was renamed to DevtoolsSelectorText() to clarify that it is intended for DevTools use only and does not represent a web-exposed API. (1d8286d)

Android

  • The terminalPrivate.onProcessOutput() event, used by the ChromeOS terminal on Android, now dispatches only to the renderer that opened the process, rather than broadcasting to all renderers. (4d9c2be)
  • TalkBack table navigation has been improved by allowing focus to move directly to a cell's primary interactive element (e.g., a single link) instead of the gridcell container. (dceb6b0)
  • A crash in the Android PDF toolbar was fixed, which was caused by floating-point rounding errors when calculating zoom levels. (a09c86b)

iOS

  • App startup performance was optimized by deferring the initialization of the Tab Grid UI until it is actually needed or the UI thread is idle. (0f67083)
  • A redundant Secure Enclave availability check was disabled during GetTokenIDs() to prevent hangs and crashes on some devices. (cec6c7f)
  • A UI flashing issue when scanning a credit card was fixed by presenting the camera view directly instead of first showing an empty card details page. (513ef0c)

Internals & Refactoring

  • A breaking change that made media::VideoColorSpace immutable was reverted due to build failures. The class members remain mutable for now. (4506bdd) (a1c5dcc)
  • A significant internal refactoring was completed to replace raw integer process IDs with the strongly-typed content::ChildProcessId in ProcessMap and its callers, enhancing type safety. (ab62ab0)
  • Backend infrastructure was added to chrome://flags to support a future import/export feature for flags. (b973615)
  • A "Listen to this page" item was added to the desktop context menu, controlled by the kImprovedReadAloud feature flag. (021e219)

2026-04-26

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-26

Overview

This digest covers 17 commits from refs/heads/main@{#(1620749)} to refs/heads/main@{#(1620852)}. The analysis identified one major breaking change in the Omnibox UI presenter interface, alongside updates to Android UI, experimental features, performance, and internal tooling.

UI & UX

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The base class for Omnibox popups, OmniboxPopupPresenterBase, has received significant interface changes. The virtual function ShouldDeferUntilVisualStateReady was changed from returning a bool to std::optional<base::TimeDelta> and made pure virtual. Additionally, a new pure virtual function, ShouldDetachWebContentsOnHide, was introduced. These changes will break any custom implementations that derive from this class, requiring them to update function signatures and implement the new method. (c708812)
  • On Android, a new DelegatingActionView interface has been introduced to allow views to delegate the binding of action properties to a different target view. Other Android UI changes include the addition of a new tab button to the bottom bar and adjustments to the page info menu logic. (2d9de5c) (4575a45) (83d2af8)
  • Minor UI fixes and updates include a fix for selection highlights in the iOS translate infobar, migration of voice search metrics on the New Tab Page, and visual tweaks to the experimental Contextual Tasks feature. (296e0c2) (a2652ff) (ecf2c1f) (27ab255)

Features

  • The feature flag kIPHPlusAddressFirstSaveFeature, related to an In-Product Help bubble for the Plus Addresses feature, has been removed. Work on the experimental Glic triggering feature continues with refactoring to wait for client readiness before handling messages. (82f1866) (7727e35)

Performance & Refactoring

  • Internal refactoring efforts include reducing unnecessary string copies in accessibility code by using string_view and base::JoinString, and general code cleanup in the Send Tab To Self component. (6de91e4) (4901bca)

Infrastructure & Tooling

  • To aid in debugging installation issues, diagnostic logging has been added to the IsIdenticalFileHierarchy function in the Windows installer. Other infrastructure changes include extending a stability metric's expiry on iOS, updating translation files, and routine updates to OWNERS files. (9d390f2) (416bd53) (5dd4f2f) (054aa78) (4004ac2)

2026-04-25

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-25

Overview

This digest covers 55 commits, highlighting several major behavioral changes to the Popover, WebGL, Geolocation, and Web App Manifest APIs. Key updates include the removal of an iOS-specific Autofill API, security enhancements for Live Captions and the Geolocation service, and numerous bug fixes across rendering, UI, and media components.

Web Platform & APIs

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The Popover API implementation has been updated to align with recent specification changes. This introduces stricter re-entrancy protection, throwing a DOMExceptionCode::kInvalidStateError if showPopover() is called during an existing show/hide operation. Additionally, popover=auto elements opened within a popover=hint will now be 'downgraded' to hint behavior instead of throwing an error. Web content relying on the previous, more lenient behavior may break. (712f6f0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Web App Manifest validation is now stricter. The browser process will now enforce that start_url, id, scope, share_target.action, file_handlers.action, and protocol_handlers.url are all same-origin with the document. Manifests with cross-origin URLs in these fields will now be rejected, which may break web apps that previously relied on the more lenient validation. (0b080ce)
  • The WebTransport API now supports the congestionControl hint as a constructor option, allowing developers to suggest a preference for 'throughput' or 'low-latency' algorithms, aligning with the W3C specification. (7faefb2)
  • Corrected multiple bugs in HTML forms, including a fix for updating <selectedcontent> in listboxes (87144c6512872f082d6265584bec96e0287d5aeb), ensuring :valid/:invalid pseudo-classes update correctly for <select multiple size=1> (6819a106c96188395dda3c08050ab97185b6ac9d), and preventing a crash by disconnecting the SelectMutationObserver when a <select> element is removed from the document (36ab6e149a627aa217e8de7f882442a5f23220bd). (87144c6) (6819a10) (36ab6e1)
  • The HTML parser's fast path has been fixed to correctly handle nested <li> elements, aligning with a patch from WebKit. (c96815f)
  • Resolved threading violations and race conditions in the Android implementation of the WebShare API, improving stability during file transfers. (5678c0f)

Security & Privacy

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The Geolocation API's behavior has changed to mitigate a privacy risk. The 15-minute throttle on frequent requests for approximate locations will now persist even if all clients disconnect and reconnect. This prevents malicious sites from bypassing the throttle to reconstruct a precise location from multiple approximate fixes. Applications relying on the throttle resetting on disconnect will be affected. (b76dfb0)
  • Fixed a JSON injection and out-of-bounds read vulnerability in the Live Caption translation feature. The code now uses structured JSON generation (base::WriteJson) instead of StringPrintf to prevent malicious peers from injecting arbitrary JSON or causing a denial of service. (a1ab0fb)
  • The Chrome Elevation Service on Windows now uses an explicitly system-locked secure temporary directory (base::GetSecureTempDirectory()) for its recovery child process, providing defense-in-depth against potential insecure C:\Windows\Temp configurations. (3297b1b)

GPU & Graphics

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: WebGL behavior has been changed for spec compliance. Attempting to relink a GL program while transform feedback is active will now fail and generate a GL_INVALID_OPERATION error. Previously, this operation may have succeeded, leading to state desynchronization. WebGL applications that relink programs during active transform feedback will need to be updated. (8617532)
  • A race condition in the Windows hardware video encoders (D3D12 and Media Foundation) has been fixed. Read access on a VideoImageRepresentation is now correctly maintained for the entire duration of an encode operation, preventing potential heap corruption from concurrent write access. (28ec09a)
  • Fixed a bug where transform feedback could be accidentally left active when switching to a newly created GL context, by ensuring the state restorer is properly initialized first. (f41b46e)
  • Support for backdrop-filter images has been added to the HTML-in-canvas drawing feature, allowing these effects to be correctly rendered when using drawImage() with an HTML element. (27dafe4)

Autofill & Omnibox

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The PlusAddressEmailOverride feature has been removed. This includes the removal of kPlusAddressEmailOverride from the AutofillSnackbarType enum on Android and, more significantly, the removal of the showPlusAddressEmailOverrideNotification method from the public AutofillClientIOSBridge protocol. iOS embedders using this method will need to remove it from their implementations. (1fd3309)
  • The abandoned In-Product Help feature for Plus Address creation suggestions (kIPHPlusAddressCreateSuggestionFeature) and all its associated code have been removed. (292966b)

iOS

  • Internal sign-in logic on iOS has been refactored, moving EPC (External Privacy Context) fetching from SigninAccountCapabilitiesSceneAgent to the centralized SystemIdentityManager. (f468cf4)

UI & Accessibility

  • An accessibility bug has been fixed where screen readers would not announce an updated tab group name. The accessible name for tabs within a group is now properly updated when the group's title changes. (fb35a0d)
  • The behavior of the Android framebust message now matches desktop: the message is now correctly dismissed on all cross-document main frame navigations. (589cfed)
  • Fixed an accessibility tree consistency failure related to <br> elements that have DOM children added via script. The logic now correctly marks the node as dirty to ensure the accessibility tree is updated. (84f83aa)

Developer Experience & Tooling

  • A new test helper, content::ReadyForInputObserver, has been added to help write more reliable browser tests. It allows tests to wait until a frame's input router is active before sending synthetic input, reducing flakiness caused by the kDropInputEventsWhilePaintHolding feature. (4398d46)
  • Several Extensions API tests (SystemStorageApiTest, SocketsTcpServerApiTest) have been migrated from the deprecated "app shell" environment to run as part of the standard chrome/browser_tests suite, improving test reliability and realism. (dc4393c) (0aeb02c)
  • The check-std-ranges-pipe-operator clang plugin option has been turned into a no-op, as part of a cleanup to eventually remove the option entirely. (b6dc473)

2026-04-24

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-24

Overview

On this day, 717 commits were merged to the main branch, with 541 being identified as relevant to developers. This summary covers a significant number of breaking API changes across the UI framework, WebRTC, prefetching, and core content APIs. Major refactoring efforts continue in the compositor, build system, and browser componentization. The first commit was c55d041505a613bca037523002843de2f7d646e4 and the last was 5d155b4bce588dba996810da80cf4c00632293f8.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The core compositor class cc::LayerTreeHostImpl has been refactored. Its public static factory method Create() was removed, and the implementation was split into ClientLayerTreeHostImpl and VizLayerTreeHostImpl. Embedders who directly instantiate this class will need to update their code. (9d32df7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The content::public::browser::WebContents interface no longer has the GetCaptureHandleConfig() method. This functionality has been moved to Page, and the corresponding delegate method was changed from SetCaptureHandleConfig to OnCaptureHandleConfigUpdate. (359ad9a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The PrefetchContainer::Observer interface has been updated. The OnGotInitialEligibility and OnPrefetchCompletedOrFailed methods no longer pass eligibility and completion status as parameters. Observers must now retrieve this data directly from the PrefetchContainer object. (219dedd) (56b1776)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The ui/views bubble API has two breaking changes: BubbleDialogDelegate::SetHighlightedButton has been removed in favor of SetHighlightedElement, and the factory method BubbleDialogDelegate::CreateBubble now requires an explicit Widget::InitParams::Ownership parameter. (b382a3d) (fa368f1)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public Java interface SidePanelContainerCoordinator.init() on Android now requires a SidePanelCoordinatorAndroid parameter to support bidirectional communication. (ad6a6ec)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The PeerConnectionTrackerHostObserver interface has been updated. The OnPeerConnectionSessionIdSet method now requires a base::OnceClosure callback, which will break existing observer implementations. (52749a3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The PasswordManagerDriver mojom interface has removed the ShowPasswordSuggestions method. Embedders must migrate to using AskForValuesToFill on the AutofillDriver interface. (8635789)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The blink::String::Number<T>() template method has been removed and replaced with explicit overloads for standard integer types. Code using this template with other types (e.g., short) will no longer compile. (52798a5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The AccountInfo::Builder has a significant behavioral change. When the kGaiaAccountIdEnforcement feature is enabled, account_id is now derived from gaia_id, and calls to SetAccountId() are ignored. (0029f3a)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On iOS, a new virtual method WillShutDown() has been added to the FullscreenBrowserAgentObserver interface, requiring all implementers to add it to their class definition. (32fe61b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public headers have been changed, breaking includes and path lookups: DIR_INTERNAL_PLUGINS was removed from chrome_paths.h; shelf-related prefs were moved to ash_pref_names.h; leak_detection_delegate_interface.h was renamed; and the RTC_USE_H264 build flag was removed from Blink's public config. (46850ff) (c4dca43) (edd9f64) (e81b84e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public C-API for the On-Device Model Service, services/on_device_model/ml/chrome_ml_api.h, has changed the signature for the GetTokenizerParams function. (db585a0)

Web Platform & Rendering

  • For Media Source Extensions (MSE), non-IDR I-frames with SEI recovery points in H.264 streams are now promoted to keyframes. This fixes seeking issues in open-GOP content for players like hls.js. (53b9408)
  • Content Security Policy for CSS Modules is now stricter: <style type=module> elements will now enforce the script-src policy in addition to style-src. (fa5ca80)
  • Numerous fixes were landed for CSS Grid Masonry, addressing crashes and incorrect layout behavior, particularly with subgrids. (715a527) (d8ff8bc) (cd6ba4d)
  • Performance of ContainerNode::ReplaceChildren() has been improved by using a single RemoveChildren() call, reducing style recalculation overhead. (1c6a39e)
  • On Windows, local font lookup is now routed through the FontDataService when the feature is enabled, part of a migration away from the legacy DWriteFontProxy interface. (01a0932) (6c6ce5d)
  • The InputVizard feature for improved input handling is now enabled by default on Android. (b1f0797)
  • A race condition in WebGL has been addressed by ensuring element array data is read exactly once. (013d0c8)

UI & Features

  • The WebUITabStrip feature, which was behind a disabled-by-default flag, has been completely removed from the codebase. (4e27221)
  • On Android, a new JNI method has been added to Tab to allow Java code to asynchronously query the memory footprint of a tab's renderer, intended for use in UI like hover cards. (fb82a78)
  • On iOS, a new runtime flag, delayLoadingResources, has been added to CWVEarlyInitFlags to allow deferring resource bundle initialization, improving startup performance for WebView clients. (d2cf47f)
  • The PDF viewer's Ink Signatures feature received a new private API, chrome.pdfViewerPrivate.getTextInfo(), to improve font handling performance. (262b57c) (dd6626c)
  • The Page Summary feature and its associated UI elements have been removed from Android, including public constants in FeatureConstants.java. (77ddc42) (59ca8a4)

Services & APIs

  • The SamplingHeapProfiler and its mojo interface now support a Stop() method, allowing clients like Electron to safely toggle the heap profiler at runtime without crashing the service. (f106c51)
  • A new API has been added to SessionSyncService to support storing tab screenshots, likely for use in features like tab resumption. (a611ade)
  • The OneTimeCodeFieldParser has been integrated into Autofill's form parsing heuristics to improve the detection of One-Time Password (OTP) fields. (9097ee6) (266913f)
  • The Send Tab To Self feature has been enhanced to use Autofill's form and field signatures for more reliable form filling on the receiving device. (ad9a1e9) (3dbb736)

Security & Stability

  • Multiple Use-After-Free (UAF) vulnerabilities have been addressed in RenderFrameHostImpl::ExitFullscreen(), WidgetBaseInputHandler, the Presentation API, and Bluetooth connection handling on macOS. (f2c3442) (d3038a7) (959627a) (cc8c915)
  • The tab freezing policy now uses base::LiveTicks instead of base::TimeTicks for its unfreeze timer. This prevents the timer from advancing while the system is suspended, avoiding a "thundering herd" of tabs being unfrozen simultaneously upon system resume. (162b410)
  • A dangling pointer in update_client::Component was fixed by switching from base::raw_ref to base::WeakPtr in callbacks, preventing crashes during component updates. (d0b245d)
  • Input validation was added to the accessibility system to prevent the renderer from sending reserved or browser-specific AXNodeID values, preventing potential collisions. (65fba3d)
  • On iOS, scheme validation was added for auto-generated search engines to prevent potential Universal Cross-Site Scripting (UXSS) vulnerabilities. (c1bb5bb)

Build & Infrastructure

  • Significant effort was made to modularize core browser components by moving sources out of the monolithic //chrome/browser:browser target, including //chrome/browser/signin and //chrome/browser/navigation_predictor. (3f51165) (5ea02e3)
  • The monolithic generate_bindings_all build action was split into 11 per-type actions, enabling parallel execution and reducing build times. (f7c2ded)
  • A new feature has been added to PartitionAlloc to reserve a "zero segment" at the beginning of the user-space address space. This is a prerequisite for the V8 Sandbox. (9924b55)
  • Major dependencies libc++ and perfetto have been rolled forward, incorporating numerous upstream changes. (612b748) (7326987)

2026-04-23

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-23

Overview

This digest covers 646 commits on the main branch, with 489 identified as relevant to developers. The changes span from (c2744eb) to (6523f9d). A significant number of breaking changes affect core services, platform-specific APIs, and graphics, driven by ongoing modularization and API modernization efforts.

Core, Services, and Build System

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The //chrome/browser/sessions component has been modularized into public API (sessions), implementation (impl), and test support targets. Downstream builds must update their dependencies to use the new public //chrome/browser/sessions target instead of referencing internal headers. (b904862)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The StartupTracingController has been moved from content/browser to services/tracing/public/cpp for broader reuse. The global singleton accessor GetInstance() has been removed, and the class is now owned by content::BrowserMainLoop. Code relying on the global singleton will need to be updated. (db2fbde)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A libc++ roll removes the public header <float.h>. Any code directly including this header will fail to compile and must be updated. (ffd3856)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Ash-specific preference name constants have been moved from the common chrome/common/pref_names.h to a new, specific header ash/webui/help_app_ui/help_app_prefs.h. Code outside of ash that used these constants will fail to compile without updating the include path. (a85965e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public helper function FindBrowserWithGroup has been removed from browser_finder.h. Callers must now implement this logic manually, for example by iterating through active browsers. (cf0a15d)

Security and Privacy

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public UnexportableKeyService mojo interface has been updated. Methods like GenerateSigningKey now use role-specific key identifiers (e.g., UnexportableSigningKeyId), and GetAllSigningKeysForGarbageCollectionSlowlyAsync was renamed to GetAllKeysForGarbageCollectionSlowlyAsync. Clients of this security API will need to be updated. (c84ea43) (234b233) (bf73c0c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The permissions::PermissionRequestData struct has been refactored to be a data-only object, removing its PermissionResolver member. This changes its constructor and usage pattern, requiring all call sites to be updated. (35ad82c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public LeakDetectionCheck::Start virtual method signature has changed to require a LeakDetectionCallback. The factory method TryCreateLeakCheck has also been updated, breaking existing implementations and call sites for the password leak detection feature. (43a05b0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The CertVerifierServiceFactory::UpdateNetworkTime Mojo interface signature has changed to include a network_time_uncertainty parameter. Callers of this interface will need to be updated. (823df87)
  • A security check was added to the File System Access API to prevent a compromised renderer from monitoring files it shouldn't have access to by ensuring the site associated with the access token matches the current site. (12f2a15)

Graphics and Rendering

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Several core graphics APIs have been modernized to use base::span for buffer handling instead of raw pointers, improving memory safety. This breaks the constructor for cc::PaintOpWriter and the signatures for GLES2Implementation::TexSubImage2DImpl and TexSubImage3DImpl. (ddd0973) (2d8635c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The mojom::GpuService::EstablishGpuChannel Mojo interface has been changed to support a new, faster GPU channel initialization path. The method now requires a mojo::ScopedMessagePipeHandle, and the callback signature is different, breaking direct callers. (221b0ba)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The return type of the public WebGPU method GPUDevice::GetFormattedLabel() has been changed from std::string to blink::String, which will break any code that directly calls this method and expects the previous type. (a3313e4)

Blink and Web Platform

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The virtual method OnMainFrameIntersectionChanged in the public PageLoadMetricsObserver interface is renamed to OnMainFrameDocumentIntersectionChanged. Embedders inheriting from this class must update their method overrides. (b269479)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The WebSensorProvider::GetSensor Mojo interface signature has changed. It now requires a bool user_gesture parameter, breaking existing implementations that call this Generic Sensor API interface. (5fbd61a)
  • The CSS gap-decorations inset properties were renamed from [edge|interior] to [cap|junction] to align with the latest CSSWG resolution on the developing specification. (576d789)
  • Overscroll behavior properties are now reflected on HTMLElement, allowing for easier feature detection and scripting of overscroll-behavior properties. (2e0e24d)
  • The BackForwardCachePauseMicrotasks feature was enabled by default, which should improve BFCache behavior by pausing microtask execution for pages in the cache. (7f5cf31)

UI and Features

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: DevTools-related methods (e.g., IsDevToolsBrowser, GetDevToolsWindow) have been removed from the core public BrowserWindow interface. Embedders must now use the new DevtoolsUIController to access this functionality. (c9a8aa7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public method BrowserWindow::ShowQRCodeGeneratorBubble has been removed. Embedders must now use the new qrcode_generator::QRCodeWindowController to show the QR code bubble. (d383d4e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The "Glass Toolbar" feature and its associated code and flag (kEnableGlassToolbar) have been removed. Any configurations or code relying on this UI feature will be affected. (0679b56)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The signature of the public method Host::instance_metrics() has been changed from returning a pointer (GlicInstanceMetrics*) to a reference (GlicInstanceMetrics&), which will break any code calling this method. (6451866)
  • A bug causing duplicate pinned tabs to appear on startup has been resolved. (82325a5)

Extensions

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The MimeHandlerStreamManager API has been refactored with generic terminology (e.g., IsPdfExtensionHost is now IsExtensionHost) and moved to extensions/browser/mime_handler. This breaks callers using the old PDF-specific method names. (371cec5) (35639b0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The command-line switch --enable-unsafe-extension-debugging has been removed. Automation or build scripts relying on this flag will fail. This capability is now implicitly enabled for extensions installed via the Chrome DevTools Protocol. (3e47d0a)
  • A race condition that could lead to duplicate service workers for a single extension was fixed by improving version ID checking during worker initialization. (efa90ae)

Android

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A change in JNI Zero no longer includes default_conversions.h by default. Android embedders who implicitly relied on these conversions being available will experience build failures and must now include the header manually. (8ec304d)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: As part of a larger refactoring, ConsentLevel.SYNC has been removed from the public Java ConsentLevel enum and the public IdentityMutator.setPrimaryAccount method has been removed, replaced by a testing-only equivalent. This breaks code relying on the direct link between sync and the primary account. (b71b759) (5360b9e)
  • A bug in WebView where destroying an AwContents with a pending JavaScript dialog could lead to a permanently blocked renderer process has been fixed. The destructor now ensures all pending callbacks are run with a failure response. (7b513bf)

iOS

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Several public methods on the AuthenticationService that accepted a ConsentLevel parameter (e.g., HasPrimaryIdentity, GetPrimaryIdentity) have been removed. Embedders on iOS must update to the versions without this parameter as part of the ongoing sign-in refactoring. (c2453a6)
  • In-process Perfetto tracing is now enabled for developer profiling builds on iOS, improving performance analysis capabilities. (a02fdf9)

Sign-in and Identity

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public boolean field is_child_account has been removed from the GaiaAuthConsumer::ClientOAuthResult struct in google_apis/gaia/. Code that constructs or inspects this struct will break and should migrate to using Account Capabilities. (8165306)

Autofill

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The kCreditCardFull value has been removed from the public accessibility_annotator::EntryType enum. Code switching on or referencing this enum value will no longer compile and should be updated to handle the default kCreditCardNumber case. (83ac275)
  • A potential security bypass in the Autofill refill mechanism was fixed by preventing sensitive credit card fields from being filled without re-authentication. (426b928)

2026-04-22

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-22

Overview

This daily summary for the main branch on 2026-04-22 covers 649 commits, with 479 identified as relevant to developers. The changes span from the first commit d80eb100687de7caeb714de06922e98b1949b68d to the last d8b766aca6cf560a969aa30424d6f1745b9acd65. This period includes a large number of breaking changes due to significant API and build system refactoring.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The long-standing BrowserList API (browser_list.h) has been completely removed. The public API surface of browser_finder.h was also reduced with the removal of FindBrowserWithID, FindBrowserWithWindow, FindBrowserWithActiveWindow, and FindBrowserWithUiElementContext. Embedders must migrate to newer mechanisms like BrowserWindowInterfaceIterator. (499cfae) (ba141b8) (af10f4e)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A major build system refactoring moved browser and application lifetime headers (e.g., application_lifetime.h, browser_shutdown.h) from the monolithic //chrome/browser target to a new //chrome/browser/lifetime target. Embedders must explicitly add a dependency on the new target. (94c8534)
