  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Web Feed feature has been completely removed. This deletes numerous C++ classes, Java bridges (WebFeedBridge), feature flags (kWebFeedAwareness), and public API methods like FeedActionDelegate.openWebFeed(). (c292b11) (1a47ae7)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The pure virtual method GetURLLoaderFactory() has been removed from the public Profile interface and moved to its parent content::BrowserContext class. Code calling this method on a Profile object will no longer compile. (5436f47)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public Java methods have been removed from the Sync and Signin services on Android, including SyncService.isSyncFeatureEnabled(), SyncService.hasSyncConsent(), and SigninManager.revokeSyncConsent(). (f42b9fc) (0639353) (960d5db)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The Autofill "Plus Address" feature has been removed. This includes the removal of GetPlusAddressDelegate() from the AutofillClient interface and related SuggestionType enum values. (ff574ae) (d65a964)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The default behavior of ContentBrowserClient::IsFullCookieAccessAllowed now returns false for storage keys created with a nonce, restricting cookie access in sandboxed or isolated contexts. This may affect embedder cookie handling. (7042a41)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The signature for WebAppInstallationAcceptanceCallback has changed to support decoupling PWA installation from its launch. Callbacks now receive an additional closure to trigger the post-install launch action. (2a13b24)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public constructor for TabMenuModel now requires a TabStripModel* parameter, breaking existing instantiations. (37ca7f3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING In Blink, the PathBuilder::CurrentPath() method now returns a Path by value instead of a const Path& to prevent use-after-free bugs. This is a public API signature change. (f3b97ba)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING On Android, the default HTTP status code for navigations without response headers has changed from 200 to 0. This affects the NavigationHandleObserver interface and may impact error handling logic. (ca8ad98)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The JNI generation tooling no longer allows parameters for the @CalledByNative Java annotation. All instances of @CalledByNative("...") must be updated to @CalledByNative. (30d45a3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The google_toolbox_for_mac third-party dependency has been removed. Embedders that transitively depended on this library will experience build failures. (bcd5847)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public API of AimEligibilityService was altered by removing the virtual method GetCountryCode(). Subclasses that override this method will no longer compile. (6ce8693)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Public methods for showing "send tab to self" bubbles have been removed from the BrowserWindow interface. This functionality is now exposed through a dedicated BrowserWindowFeature. (ab08725)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The build_tflite_with_nnapi build flag has been removed. Embedders relying on this flag to enable the TFLite NNAPI delegate will have their builds broken. (6caa8b6)

Security

  • DevTools has been hardened by disallowing devtools:// access via Target.createTarget for untrusted clients and restricting file:// resource loading from remote frontends. A new --allow-unsafe-devtools-remote-file-loading flag is available as an escape hatch. (28c26ea) (8e5202f)
  • A Time-of-Check to Time-of-Use (TOCTOU) vulnerability in DataObjectItem::GetAsFile was fixed by adding sequence number validation for clipboard items. (e59c8af)
  • A vulnerability where third-party SharedWorkers could bypass SameSite cookie restrictions when creating WebSocket connections has been fixed. (2b49183)
  • Security for Mojo IPC was hardened on Windows by improving remote process ID detection and adding an option to verify the server process's integrity level to prevent named pipe squatting attacks. (0e083d3)
  • A Use-After-Free in WebViewImpl::Minimize was fixed by using a weak pointer for a delayed task, and another in Page::OrdinaryPages() was fixed by copying the collection before iteration. (b1a2341) (7428099)
  • A Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) vulnerability in Cast was fixed by adding URL validation for media image URLs received from Cast devices. (16b9265)

Blink & Web Platform

  • The MergeFixedLayers and MergeStickyLayers rendering optimizations are now enabled by default to improve performance. (d80eb10)
  • The initiatorUrl for ResourceTiming is now enabled for dedicated and shared workers, providing more granular attribution for fetched resources. (dc78e7e)
  • A bug in AudioBufferSourceNode was fixed to correctly handle cases where start() is called without a source buffer attached, aligning with the Web Audio API specification. (ab5a5fd)
  • SVG filters using userSpaceOnUse will now correctly resolve percentage values against the viewport of the element where the filter is used, not where it was defined. (de5c226)
  • The <selectedcontent> element was updated to support <select multiple>, allowing it to clone and display content from all selected options. (3606758)
  • Video captions will now dynamically update when OS caption settings change on Windows, improving accessibility. (f593405)

Progressive Web Apps (PWA)

  • A graceful recovery flow was implemented for when the Web App Database is corrupted or fails to open. Instead of crashing, the browser will now attempt to salvage data, show a profile error dialog, and reset the PWA system. (6ffaab9)

Desktop & UI

  • The UI for the "Tabs from Other Devices" side panel has been implemented, showing a device picker and a list of tabs. (4f374b7)
  • Support for context menus on pinned action toolbar buttons was implemented as part of the WebUI toolbar migration. (1b3a82e)
  • A significant internal refactoring moved the WebContentsModalDialogManager delegate logic out of the Browser class and into a new BrowserWindowModalDialogDelegate. (083c2a8)
  • The chrome://policy page has been modernized by porting its promotion banner and export logic from legacy WebUI messages to Mojo. (e3cc2f5)

Android

  • The TranslatePrefs class was updated to store a list of recent target languages instead of a single one, in preparation for a richer language selection UI. (c691383)
  • Reload support was implemented for the native PDF page viewer, guarded by the InlinePdfV2 feature flag. (5dd34c7)
  • The media notification icon was changed from a sound icon to the Chrome icon to avoid user confusion. (9ab824f)
  • The list of supported media codecs for the MediaLauncherActivity was explicitly enumerated to prevent attempts to open unsupported file types. (17d2fdf)

iOS

  • Enterprise policy checks were implemented for passkey creation, ensuring compliance with PasswordManagerEnabled and other related policies. (dbab372)
  • Logic has been implemented to protect the Tab Grid from screenshots based on enterprise policies. (1ebf897)
  • The AuthenticationService API was updated to add new methods that do not require a ConsentLevel argument, as part of a larger cleanup effort. (e6b6aed)
  • A race condition was fixed in the Payment Methods UI that could cause unresponsiveness during rapid tapping. (24a6fcf)

Internals & Code Health

  • A new helper, base::WeakValueTable, was introduced for managing thread-safe shared caches of weakly-owned values. (dd21b7b)
  • A new public key encryption API (crypto::encrypt::Encrypt/Decrypt) was added to the crypto library. (34f8ea3)
  • A new helper function, ScaleByMemoryLimit, was introduced in base/memory_coordinator to provide a safe way to scale memory cache sizes based on system memory limits. (64428d6)
  • The media app accessibility code was extracted from the monolithic chrome/browser target into its own source_set to improve build times and dependency management. (cf9b018)
  • A large number of files were updated to migrate comments referencing old crbug.com issue IDs to the new issue tracker format. (abac920)

2026-04-21

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-21

Overview

This day saw 684 commits, with 496 deemed relevant to developers. The changes span from significant breaking API removals in the Remoting, Compositor, and Sync components to major feature cleanups like the removal of the WebNN DirectML backend and parts of the Web Feed feature. Key improvements include new capabilities for Android multi-window management, performance enhancements for the Omnibox, and security fixes for download resumption and renderer-initiated crashes. First commit: 054c47b1b245e62ccc13ccb3e6f9209eab288a35, last commit: 69a4fe10c99acd66f172d6770d5d5a25f35747d5.

API & Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The legacy (non-WebRTC) remoting protocol has been significantly dismantled. Public classes BufferedSocketWriter, MessageReader, MessageDecoder, and the core VideoEncoder/VideoDecoder base classes have been removed from the public API. Embedders using the legacy Chromotocol protocol will be broken. (2b28768) (9cc0984)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The core compositor class cc::OwnedLayerImplList has had several public, non-const accessor methods (e.g., begin, end, at) removed to enforce stricter API usage. Direct iteration or modification of the layer list by embedders is no longer possible and will require code changes. (c5db8df)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: In Blink's layout engine, individual margin accessors like MarginTop() and MarginLeft() have been removed from the public API of LayoutBox and LayoutBoxModelObject. Developers must now use the new MarginOutsets() method, which returns all margins in a single struct. (6cb32f8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public Java interface SyncService on Android has been cleaned up, removing the isSyncFeatureActive() and getSetupInProgressHandle() methods. Embedders must adapt to new ways of checking sync state. (6e99a22) (24cc34c)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: The public GPU API class SharedImageInfo has been refactored to inherit from SharedImageMetadata. This changes how embedders access metadata fields (e.g., info.meta.size must be updated to info.size), requiring code updates. (722157f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: Multiple public APIs had breaking signature or name changes. This includes FaviconHelper methods on Android adding a new parameter (ec7ce06e6d2a8f7011b9340696b013b8e39a74be), AccessibilityAnnotatorDatabase::DeleteContentAnnotation now taking a span (585ef24a33406f3b849c656ff81e6e82c4a81216), Android's PopupCreator::createPopupWindow being renamed (fadf008d299bb0a5e24c96bb3d5793dff82bbc22), and enum members in wtf::text::TextCodec::FlushBehavior being renamed (3b9e904f0510c9f89ba07eb2dae1b554fd8b05a4). (ec7ce06) (585ef24) (fadf008) (3b9e904)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING BREAKING: A wide range of public APIs were removed. This includes: parsers from components/web_package (f64dbc334021a2cf51236f6061486996588a3f9e), ContextMenuDelegate::BuildMenuAsync() (378dd7bc488cc6f16850ed0abb8d6c08efb10933), the kLetAudioDescriptionFinish enum value from WebMediaPlayer::PauseReason (753802242b0872e8300c0871297fa912ee43c25f), methods from content::PrefetchContainer (4ec75a6c68768539df4d7c86795e40792b68a2ca), and an IPC from autofill_agent.mojom (2b232a587a7f0aed5f98def610333dc482a1b679). (f64dbc3) (378dd7b) (7538022) (4ec75a6) (2b232a5)

Feature Removals

  • The DirectML (DML) backend for the WebNN API has been completely removed. The ONNX Runtime (ORT) is now the sole supported path for DML on Windows. (ccff00c)
  • The 'Plus Addresses' feature is being deprecated. The settings entry point has been removed from the Android UI, and all associated PlusAddressMetrics have been deleted from the codebase. (089f13f) (1d3780c)
  • The ongoing removal of the Web Feed feature continues, with this batch removing the post-follow dialog and related metrics. (84bb07d)
  • The experimental Page Summary adaptive toolbar button, an AI-powered feature, has been removed along with its associated services. (f71a274)

Blink & Web Platform

  • The CSS image-animation property now correctly propagates from the root element to the viewport, fixing a bug where animations on the root's background would not play. (121e378)
  • Initial infrastructure has been added for the new CSS property text-decoration-skip-spaces. While the rendering logic is not yet implemented, the property is now parsed. (9e279b5)
  • Parsing for SVGStringList has been updated to correctly handle leading whitespace and empty items, improving compliance with HTML microsyntax specifications. (c73305f)

Android

  • Significant refactoring of multi-window and tab reparenting logic now allows for creating a new browser window from an existing WebContents. This improves flexibility for embedders managing multiple windows. (edfa5f2) (6e38ae5)
  • The BottomSheet component now supports a RESIZE_CONTENT height mode, enabling content to dynamically resize as the user drags the sheet. This was adopted by the Tab Bottom Sheet for a more fluid UX. (0c80f24) (6c2cc02)
  • WebView's cookie selection logic for intercepted requests was fixed to correctly respect Same-Site restrictions, preventing potential cross-site information leaks. (7b41029)

Security

  • A potential crash in VideoCaptureHost, caused by a compromised renderer calling ReleaseBuffer twice, is now handled gracefully by treating it as a 'bad message' instead of a CHECK failure. (6981dd9)
  • A security flaw in download resumption that could allow hash-based blocklist checks to be bypassed has been fixed. The hash state is now correctly reset when a download is resumed. (fc030cd)
  • When fetching a variations seed, X-Country and X-Geo-Level-1 headers are now only trusted over an HTTPS connection to prevent potential forgery via insecure networks. (536eb1d)
  • A Universal Cross-Site Scripting (UXSS) vulnerability in Reading Mode via the ttsEngine API was fixed by properly sanitizing language strings. (eb38552)

Desktop & Cross-Platform UI

  • A new TopChromeWebUIDialogDelegate has been introduced to simplify the creation of top-level WebUI dialogs, reducing boilerplate code. The default browser modal was refactored to use this new framework. (f14a8c0) (0e80a62)
  • To reduce latency on the critical path, database updates for the AutocompleteActionPredictor are now deferred to a low-priority background task. (5d63702)
  • On Wayland, window decorations will now correctly paint in an 'active' state during move and resize operations, improving visual consistency. This was achieved by separating the paint state from keyboard focus. (00dbd87)

Build System & Tooling

  • The /prefetch-inputs flag is now enabled by default for lld-link on Windows, which should improve linking performance, especially during cold cache builds. (8e295b0)
  • The Windows Template Library (WTL) dependency has been updated to version 10.1.0 and is now managed via CIPD, ensuring it stays up-to-date. (3a8ef69) (c08beab)
  • Efforts to improve build times and dependency hygiene continue with the modularization of several components, including //chrome/browser/new_tab_page, //chrome/browser/ssl, and //chrome/browser/metrics. (22a94a8) (124fb31) (2968416)

2026-04-20

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-20

Overview

This summary covers 440 relevant commits out of 601 total commits in the main branch on April 20, 2026. The changes span from the first commit 46394ace988541e7481f9446e9472fb3ea420dac to the last bbad371561e34913370ececf50bc29959838b7f7.

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core & Networking: The return types of several public methods on net::URLRequest (e.g., GetTotalReceivedBytes) have been changed from int64_t to base::ByteSize, which will cause compilation errors for consumers. (8ade05b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Core & Filesystem: The default behavior of base::DeletePathRecursively on Windows has changed to no longer traverse reparse points (like directory junctions). This can silently alter the behavior of file operations for embedders who relied on the previous traversal logic. (4ca74d8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Remoting: Legacy audio components have been removed from the Remoting API, including the public headers remoting/codec/audio_encoder.h and remoting/codec/audio_decoder.h. Embedders relying on this legacy audio pipeline will experience build failures. (df08c2f)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING VIZ & Graphics: The signature of FrameSinkManagerImpl::SubmitHitTestRegionList has changed from void to bool. More importantly, the service will now disconnect clients that submit invalid hit-test data, which is a new failure mode for embedders. (b1585a0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Blink & Graphics: The stop member of the public struct blink::Gradient::ColorStop has been changed from a double to a float. This is a binary-incompatible change that will affect embedders who use this graphics utility directly. (2a25df3)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Android Sync: Several public methods have been removed from the components.sync.SyncService Java interface, including isInitialSyncFeatureSetupComplete(), hasKeepEverythingSynced(), and setSelectedTypes(). This will break embedders using the Sync-as-a-feature API. (d78abe4) (7fcfbca)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Android Features: The Web Feed feature has been removed from Android, including its associated UI components (WebFeedMainMenuItem) and APIs. This is a removal of a stable, user-facing feature. (bafb125)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Android Read Aloud: The public, though deprecated, methods getUrl() and getDescription() have been removed from PlaybackArgs.java in the Android Read Aloud module API. (52bdc97)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Autofill: The virtual method TriggerPlusAddressUserPerceptionSurvey has been removed from the public C++ interface autofill::AutofillClient. Embedders who implement this interface must remove their overrides. (2f6d1f8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING GuestView: The SlimWebView JavaScript API has been changed by replacing the allowedOrigins property with allowedOriginsParams. The internal SlimWebViewNavigationThrottle was also removed, breaking integrations that use this non-standard GuestView component. (10807a0)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Privacy & Security: The public enum PrivacySandboxService::PromptSuppressedReason has been removed. Additionally, the public Mojo interface trust_tokens.mojom has been changed, removing the custom_key_commitment and custom_issuer fields. (41e4d73) (e4f0243)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Accessibility & FIDO: The signature of the public function ui::AXUrlBaseText has been changed to accept a std::u16string_view. Separately, the signature of device::enclave::Transact has been altered to return a std::unique_ptr to manage transaction lifecycle. (d1d0797) (c42180a)

Core, Internals & Performance

  • A large-scale refactoring was performed to cache the parsed GURL for the New Tab Page, avoiding repeated parsing across components like safe_browsing, sessions, and extensions to improve performance. (ec0f7b5) (9e92c17) (9abdf92) (910f0e9)
  • A use-after-free crash in SpdyStream was fixed by adding a WeakPtr check to prevent access after a session has started draining. (6810eeb)
  • The //third_party/afl fuzzer and the ink_stroke_modeler dependency have been removed from the build. The libc++ dependency was also rolled forward. (366e55c) (14b7b21) (1a580b5)
  • Checked Span memory safety checks have been enabled in release builds for canary and dev channels to help detect memory issues earlier. (41af5c6)

Blink, VIZ & Web Platform

  • Fonts: A new Mojo API, MatchLocalFont, was added to FontDataService to handle @font-face src: local() lookups in the browser process, with an initial implementation on Windows using DirectWrite. (662a4cc)
  • Layout: The implementation of LayoutBox client and content rect getters (e.g., ClientWidth) was refactored to use data from physical fragments instead of computed style, improving accuracy, especially in paged media. (a6f646c)
  • DOM: For performance, Bloom filters for static QualifiedNames (like HTML attributes) are now precomputed at initialization, speeding up attribute lookups. (8dec1ad)
  • WebAudio: A memory leak was fixed where AudioContext and AudioWorkletGlobalScope were not being reclaimed on AudioContext.close(). The fix involves explicitly terminating messaging proxies. (89899b8)
  • IndexedDB: Logic was added to handle cases where a renderer reports a blob size that differs from its actual size, preventing potential quota and data corruption issues. (5e6a8e5)
  • The enable-clipboardchange-event feature flag was removed, as the clipboardchange event is now a stable feature. (ec86ba4)

Security & Privacy

  • Network Security: A potential stack-based buffer overflow in the ONC (Open Network Configuration) message unpacker was fixed by adding validation for HMAC length. (fa1312b)
  • Safe Browsing: A request tier system was introduced to allow different types of client-side detection requests to block or bypass each other, improving resource management. (4ff2fbb)
  • Policies: A new policy, KioskPinchToZoomAllowed, was added to give administrators control over pinch-to-zoom in Kiosk sessions. (67c0264)
  • Privacy Sandbox: The PrivacySandboxPromptEnabled policy has been deprecated as the associated user prompt is being removed from the platform. (ff025fc)

Features & Services

  • Remoting: A large amount of legacy code related to the decommissioned "Chromotocol" protocol was removed, including P2PDatagramSocket, PseudoTCP components, and various channel factories. (59bb995) (76fc706) (053d46b)
  • Extensions: The unused webrtcAudioPrivate extension API was completely removed. Separately, the chrome.appviewTag API was restricted to ChromeOS only, as Chrome Apps are deprecated on other platforms. (6f72765) (a7c9dd8)
  • Autofill: A crash was fixed when merging Autofill profiles that contain an invalid phone number country code. (333c435)
  • Sync & Sign-in: A live browser test was added to verify Device Bound Session Credentials (DBSC) registration during the sign-in flow. (b306023)

Android & iOS

  • Android: A series of commits addressed Omnibox focus behavior related to the CompositorViewHolderObscuring feature, improving stability. (8ca40ce) (d77805d) (69be62c)
  • Android: A threading issue was fixed to ensure JniPaymentApp is always destroyed on the UI thread, preventing potential crashes in the payments flow. (1338451)
  • Android: The WebSerialWiredDevicesAndroid feature, which allows web pages to connect to wired serial devices, was enabled by default. (8cb21e2)
  • iOS: A new observer callback, FullscreenDidTransition, was added to notify when fullscreen transitions are fully complete. (fde9a76)
  • iOS: Scroll position restoration for "Send Tab to Self" notifications was implemented, improving the user experience when opening shared tabs. (7252cda)

2026-04-19

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-19

Overview

This digest covers 29 commits, highlighting two major breaking changes in UI components and Chrome Remote Desktop. Other key developments include significant internal refactoring of the networking stack's disk cache, numerous test enhancements and bug fixes for the HTML canvas element, and ongoing build system modularization.

UI & Views

  • ⚠️ BREAKING A refactor of tab hover cards introduces a breaking change by replacing the HoverCardAnchorTarget::GetAnchorView() virtual method with a new pure virtual method, GetAnchor(), which returns a views::BubbleAnchor instead of a views::View*. Embedders or custom UI components subclassing HoverCardAnchorTarget must update their implementation to provide the new anchor type. (174be1f)
  • Various UI enhancements were made, including persisting the window size for auto Picture-in-Picture on Android, reordering buttons on the profile creation screen for better readability, and temporarily disabling animations on the first-run sign-in page. (b5af6f8) (e73f207) (88eebcc)

Chrome Remote Desktop

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The host setup logic on Linux has been updated, introducing a breaking change to the remoting::StartHostAsRoot function. Its signature has been changed from int StartHostAsRoot(int argc, char** argv) to int StartHostAsRoot(), removing its parameters. Scripts or tools that call this function directly will need to be updated. (24cd9c6)

Networking

  • The SQL-based disk cache backend received significant internal updates to improve asynchronous task management. New classes like SqlAsyncTaskManager and SqlTrackedSequenceBound were introduced to provide more reliable synchronization for testing purposes, ensuring that complex, multi-step operations can be tracked to completion without relying on RunUntilIdle(). (0e4d1c9) (d809804) (38c4f09)

Blink & Web Platform

  • Extensive work was done to improve the robustness of the 2D canvas implementation. This includes adding comprehensive web tests for context loss and restoration scenarios, especially when combined with GPU process loss or invalid canvas sizes. A bug was also fixed where a resource dispatcher was not correctly recreated after a context was restored, affecting desynchronized canvases. (9b65e50) (676e2fd) (70940fb) (cc55330) (3c95ea3) (0ccd8f8) (ff41cf0)
  • Regular Web Platform Test (WPT) imports bring in new tests and expectations. (17970ac) (91342bb) (a942ac1)

Autofill & Payments

  • Autofill sees several updates, including new metrics to differentiate between suggestions for cards saved externally (e.g., via Google Play) versus newly-synced cards. On iOS, the logic for displaying passport details was improved. The in-product help bubble for saved cards was also updated to mention 'Google Wallet' for brand consistency. (6fb6cb0) (d481fcf) (7ba1c54)

Build & Infrastructure

  • Ongoing modularization efforts continue, with //chrome/browser/support_tool and //chrome/browser/ntp_customization being extracted into their own source sets. The build dependency script install-build-deps.py was updated for compatibility with Debian 'sid'. (0f60250) (ba57552) (5698a61)
  • Several expired histograms were removed from the codebase, and CI was updated to run gtests on betty for a percentage of Googler CLs. (3db3a99) (2cbe2ff) (2a3b985)

Security

  • A security vulnerability was addressed in the VA-API JPEG encoder (VaapiJpegEncodeAccelerator) by adding an explicit bounds check to prevent a potential out-of-bounds write during the encoding process. (c24fe50)

2026-04-18

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-18

Overview

This digest covers 48 commits, highlighting a significant breaking change in the core url library. Key themes include the ongoing removal of legacy code in the Remoting component, major refactoring to eliminate ATL dependencies on Windows, and the shipment of new web platform features like CSSPseudoElement. Other changes involve accessibility improvements, bug fixes, and routine test maintenance.

Core

  • ⚠️ BREAKING Removes the deprecated IsStandard(const char* spec, const Component& scheme) overload from the public url library API. This function was marked for removal and is now gone. Embedders relying on this unsafe, char*-based function must migrate to the IsStandard(std::optional<std::string_view> scheme) overload to avoid build failures. (83cace3)

Internals & Refactoring

  • Removes legacy ICE (Interactive Connectivity Establishment) components from the Remoting stack, including IceTransport, IceTransportChannel, IceConnectionToClient, and IceConnectionToHost. This is part of a larger effort to modernize the connection protocol and remove the old "Chromotocol". (e8ee844) (14c775e)
  • Continues the large-scale effort to remove dependencies on the Windows-specific Active Template Library (ATL). This change replaces ATL security classes like CSecurityDesc and CSid with cross-platform base::win equivalents in the updater and enterprise companion components. (c7f06b9)
  • Enhances WTF::String by adding new Split() and SplitSkippingEmpty() overloads that accept a function predicate. This provides a more flexible and powerful mechanism for string splitting, replacing older, less generic implementations. (610a506)
  • Cleans up obsolete code by removing the unused manifest_update_utils files for Progressive Web Apps (PWAs), as the logic has been migrated to a modern job-based architecture. (9338d92)

Web Platform

  • Enables the CSSPseudoElement interface and the event.pseudoTarget property by default. This change ships new web-facing APIs that allow developers to interact with pseudo-elements in JavaScript. (583bc39)

Accessibility (a11y)

  • Fixes an issue on macOS where VoiceOver would incorrectly announce edits to <input type="number"> (spin button) fields. The change prevents text edit notifications for value changes made via stepping, ensuring the screen reader announces the full new value correctly. (9bfcec8)

Android

  • Corrects camera frame rotation logic for external cameras on Android. The feed from an external camera will no longer rotate with the device, fixing incorrect video orientation. (182b818)

2026-04-17

Open Standalone Page

Chromium Digest: 2026-04-17

Overview

A busy day with 763 total commits on the main branch, of which 546 were deemed relevant for this summary. The changes span from major API removals to web platform advancements and significant security hardening. The first commit of the day was (d5044d6) and the last was (9a36092).

Breaking Changes

  • ⚠️ BREAKING The legacy synchronous OSCrypt backend (components/os_crypt/sync) has been completely removed. Embedders must migrate from the public OSCrypt::EncryptString and OSCrypt::DecryptString APIs to the os_crypt/async backend. (a4cab4a) (21b80f4)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING A new virtual method, OnFedCmFederatedLogin, has been added to the public content::WebContentsObserver interface. Embedders with custom observer implementations must add this method to compile. (17d438b)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public ui::OzonePlatform interface was refactored, renaming PreEarlyInitialization() to PreSandboxStartup(). This will break custom Ozone platform implementations. (970a6d5)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public Java interface method SwipeGestureListener.SwipeHandler#isSwipeEnabled() now requires a MotionEvent parameter, breaking all existing implementations. (86a8f48)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The behavior of the stable CSS :lang() pseudo-class has been changed to no longer match elements with ill-formed lang attributes. This aligns with the CSSWG specification but may break the styling of websites that relied on the previous, more lenient behavior. (95aa378)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The navigator.credentials.get() web API will now throw a NotSupportedError when called without a credential type, aligning with the specification. This replaces the previous behavior of returning null and may break websites that relied on it. (b17a514)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Multiple public C++ APIs were removed or changed, requiring code updates for embedders. This includes the removal of net::HashValue::DeprecatedFromString, passage_embeddings::Embedding::Dimensions(), and ToolbarButtonProvider::GetAvatarToolbarButton(), and signature changes to PrePrefetchService::Create and MediaWebContentsObserver::OnMediaMetadataChanged. (104e45e) (49a6256) (6c9ccc1) (7a24f21) (1a7e135)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING Several public Java interfaces on Android were changed, breaking embedder implementations. This includes the removal of openInAppOrChromeFromCct from TabWebContentsDelegateAndroid and the renaming of the PopupIntentCreator interface to PopupCreator. (05bf5a2) (690a2ae) (a6db4e8)
  • ⚠️ BREAKING The public mojo interface blink::mojom::DevicePostureProvider was changed to remove emulation methods, and the return type of RenderWidgetHostDelegate::GetDevicePostureProvider was also changed, breaking embedder implementations. (829bf4e)

Security

  • Sandbox and IPC hardening continues. Browser-side sandbox enforcement was added for kPointerLock, and the extensions EventRouter now performs stricter validation on listener registration to prevent renderer exploits. (80ddc28) (5cca17e)
  • A potential Origin header spoofing vulnerability during cross-origin redirects was mitigated by rejecting requests where the Origin header is modified. (fd71efb)
  • On Windows, Module Tampering Protection is now enabled for Code Integrity Guard (CIG) processes to prevent unauthorized DLL injection. (60632ed)
  • A SameSite Strict cookie bypass was fixed in Android's "Preview page" feature by correctly passing the initiator origin. (62735f6)
  • On macOS, depth limits were added to bookmark pasteboard helpers to prevent stack overflow crashes from maliciously crafted clipboard data. (c5f67dc)
  • A timeout was added to the Variations Service seed fetch request to mitigate a potential attack vector that could bypass certificate expiration checks. (ad917ff)

Web Platform & Blink

  • A new performance.speculations.preloads API was added, allowing web developers to access measurement data for preloaded resources. (2f2079d)
  • HTML parser performance was significantly improved for pages with large inline scripts or styles by throttling unnecessary flushes, which previously caused O(n^2) behavior. (1e0d769)
  • The compositor will now merge pending layers for sticky and fixed position elements with compatible constraints, reducing layer count and improving performance. (dc6a5be) (73b7cd8)
  • Support for parsing declarative shadow roots in stream*HTML was implemented. (b782d6c)
  • A console warning will now be emitted when a programmatic focus call is denied by the focus-without-user-activation permissions policy, improving developer experience. (b0e008f)
  • A significant bug was fixed where click events were dropped on ephemeral pseudo-elements (e.g., :active::after) that were destroyed during event dispatch. (82777e8)

Features, UI & UX

  • The Android "Creator" page, part of the Web Feed feature, has been removed. This includes the CreatorActivity and related logic. (5653d2b)
  • The setting to show memory usage on tab hover cards has been deprecated and removed from the UI. (7df2960)
  • A new precedence-ordered registry for MIME handlers was added for extensions, allowing for more predictable handler selection. (acf2d3c)
  • On Linux, a new NativeFrameViewLinux class was introduced to provide native client-side decorations for non-browser widgets when using the GTK UI theme, improving the appearance of dialogs. (e0f759f)
  • A crash when restoring tab groups has been fixed. The issue occurred when closing a window with multiple groups and then restoring them into different windows. (e8ab45b)

Platform Integration

  • On Windows, a new Gamepad data fetcher based on the modern GameInput API was implemented, improving support for modern controllers. (ce05dce)
  • On Android, a use-after-free crash in ModalDialogWrapper was fixed. The crash occurred if a dialog was dismissed by the OS after its native counterpart was destroyed. (8f0f462)
  • On Android, a fix was implemented in WindowAndroid to correctly update its associated display when an activity is not recreated on display change, preventing incorrect UI scaling. (766e18e)
  • For enterprise reporting, a new StartupCommandLineCollector class was added to capture command-line switches used at browser startup. (380c663)
  • The Target.TargetInfo object in the Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) now exposes a parentId, providing more context for targets like dedicated workers. (96647e3)

Build & Code Health

  • A significant effort to modularize //chrome/browser continues. Multiple components, including notifications, subresource_filter, tab_group_suggestion, resource_coordinator, first_run, policy, and local_discovery, were moved out of the monolithic :browser target. (4ae8b1e) (e514a41) (2fc215c) (8ca1b4c) (0370215) (70f09b8) (1b7e251)
  • Key development dependencies were updated, including Xcode to version 17E202 and ESLint to 10.2.0. (287de9b) (df9f4e9)
  • The build system was updated to support Ubuntu 26.04. (c9042f7)
  • PaintOp serialization and deserialization APIs were modernized to use base::span instead of raw pointers, improving memory safety. (283a033)
  • An obsolete and unused file (mapped_file_bypass_mmap_posix.cc), which was a 13-year-old hack for early Android devices, was removed from the codebase. (e4763b0)
Page 1 of 2
